use_when: When building or auditing the URL structure of an ecommerce site, especially during site architecture design, platform migration, or when experiencing indexing or duplicate content issues.
avoid_when: When you do not control the URL structure (e.g., using a fully managed platform with no URL customization options) or when the site is not publicly indexed.
source_title: Ecommerce URL Structure Design Best Practices

name: Implement Dataset Structured Data for Google Dataset Search Discovery
summary: Add schema.org Dataset structured data markup to research or scientific dataset pages to improve their discoverability in Google Dataset Search.
use_when: Publishing a dataset page (life sciences, social sciences, machine learning, civic/government data, etc.) that you want discoverable in Google Dataset Search tool.
avoid_when: When the page contains values within a dataset rather than metadata about the dataset itself, or when the content is not a dataset (e.g., a general article or product page).
source_title: Dataset Structured Data – Scientific and Research Data Discovery

name: Web Hosting Migration Without URL Changes
summary: Step-by-step procedure to migrate a site to a new hosting provider or CDN while keeping all URLs intact and minimizing SEO disruption.
use_when: Changing hosting provider or moving to a CDN where all user-visible URLs remain the same and no URL restructuring is involved.
avoid_when: When the migration also involves URL changes, site restructuring, or domain changes — those require a different migration process.
source_title: Changing Your Web Hosting Without Changing URLs

name: Implement Host Carousel Structured Data with ItemList
summary: Add ItemList structured data to a summary or all-in-one page to enable host carousel rich results in Google Search, combining ItemList with a supported content type (e.g., recipes, courses, movies).
use_when: When a site has a list of related items (recipes, courses, movies, etc.) and wants to be eligible for a host carousel rich result on Google mobile search.
avoid_when: When a page contains multiple unrelated lists, or when only a single standalone item page exists without a list structure — ItemList markup should not be applied to multiple unrelated lists on the same page.
source_title: Carousel Structured Data – Host Carousels with ItemList

name: Debug Website Indexing and Content Issues Using Google Search Operators
summary: Use Google Search operators (site:, filetype:, imagesize:, src:) to diagnose indexing, file type, image size, and hotlinking issues on a website.
use_when: When you need to quickly check if specific URLs are indexed, identify spam, find specific file types, detect improperly sized images, or discover hotlinked images without access to Search Console.
avoid_when: When precise or exhaustive indexing data is needed — use the URL Inspection tool in Search Console instead, as search operators are subject to indexing and retrieval limits.
source_title: Debugging with Google Search Operators

name: Technical SEO Implementation for JavaScript Web Applications
summary: A procedure for developers to ensure JavaScript-based web applications are properly crawlable and indexable by Google Search, covering rendering, navigation, mobile compatibility, and structured data.
use_when: Building or auditing single-page applications, client-side rendered sites, or any JavaScript-heavy web application that needs to be indexed by Google Search.
avoid_when: Working with traditional server-side rendered sites where full HTML content is already present in the initial response and no JavaScript routing is used.
source_title: Developer's Guide to Search

name: Evaluating Whether to Hire an SEO Professional
summary: A structured decision process for site owners to determine if they need an SEO, what a good SEO should do, and how to identify red flags when evaluating candidates.
use_when: A site owner is considering hiring an SEO professional and needs to assess whether it is necessary and how to vet candidates appropriately.
avoid_when: The site is small with straightforward content and the owner is comfortable using free Google resources like Search Console and the SEO Starter Guide.
source_title: Do You Need an SEO

name: Implement DiscussionForumPosting Structured Data on Forum Pages
summary: Add schema.org DiscussionForumPosting markup to forum and discussion platform pages to help Google understand user-generated content and improve search appearance.
use_when: When adding structured data to forum websites or discussion platforms that host user-generated posts and replies.
avoid_when: When content is primarily authored by the website publisher or their agents, or when the site's primary structure is questions with answers (use Q&A markup instead).
source_title: Discussion Forum Structured Data – DiscussionForumPosting

name: Implement AMP Pages for Google Search Eligibility
summary: Set up AMP pages correctly so they are valid, indexable, and eligible for AMP-related Google Search features.
use_when: When building or auditing AMP pages that need to be crawled, indexed, and surfaced in Google Search with AMP-related features.
avoid_when: When the site does not use AMP or when targeting desktop-only search features, since AMP on desktop does not get search-specific features in Google Search results.
source_title: AMP on Google Search – Guidelines, How It Works, Enhancing, Validating, and Removing

name: Implement Dynamic Rendering as a JavaScript Content Workaround
summary: Set up a dynamic rendering server that detects bots and serves server-rendered HTML to crawlers while delivering the normal client-side rendered version to regular users.
use_when: JavaScript-generated content is not being indexed by search engines and a full migration to server-side rendering, static rendering, or hydration is not immediately feasible.
avoid_when: You intend to serve completely different content to crawlers versus users (which constitutes cloaking), or when a permanent scalable solution like SSR or static rendering can be implemented instead.
source_title: Dynamic Rendering as a Workaround for JavaScript Content

name: Remove AMP Pages from Google Search Results
summary: Safely decommission AMP pages and redirect traffic to canonical non-AMP pages so they no longer appear in Google Search results.
use_when: When a site is retiring its AMP implementation and needs to remove AMP pages from Google Search results without losing traffic.
avoid_when: When only temporarily hiding AMP pages or when AMP pages should remain indexed but just not featured in certain search features.
source_title: AMP on Google Search – Guidelines, How It Works, Enhancing, Validating, and Removing

name: Control Google Crawling and Indexing of a Website
summary: A structured procedure for site owners to manage how Google finds, accesses, processes, and displays their web content, covering everything from URL structure to content removal.
use_when: When launching a new site, performing a site migration, troubleshooting crawl/indexing issues, or auditing an existing site's crawl control mechanisms.
avoid_when: When the site has no existing content or is not yet ready for any search engine discovery; premature configuration may cause unintended blocking.
source_title: Crawling and Indexing – Overview

name: Implement FAQPage Structured Data for Government or Health Sites
summary: Mark up FAQ content using FAQPage schema.org structured data to qualify for rich results in Google Search, following all required property and eligibility rules.
use_when: The site is a government or health-focused authoritative website with FAQ pages where a single definitive answer is provided per question by the site itself.
avoid_when: The site allows users to submit multiple answers to a question (use QAPage instead), the FAQ content is intended for advertising purposes, or the site is not a recognized government or health authority.
source_title: FAQ Structured Data – FAQPage for Government and Health Sites

name: Controlling What Content Google Can Find and Index
summary: A decision-based procedure for site owners to block or restrict specific content from appearing in Google Search results, using one or more of four main methods depending on the desired outcome.
use_when: When a site owner needs to restrict logged-in-only content, suppress low-quality or duplicate pages, or remove already-indexed URLs from Google Search results.
avoid_when: When the goal is to improve visibility or indexing of content — this playbook is only for restriction, not promotion.
source_title: Controlling What Content Google Can Find and Index

name: Add Event Structured Data to Event Pages
summary: Implement schema.org Event structured data on event pages to make them eligible for Google's event rich results in search, displaying event name, date, location, and ticket info.
use_when: You have direct control over a website's HTML and want event pages to appear in Google's event search experience with rich result details.
avoid_when: Events are managed entirely through a third-party ticketing platform already integrated with Google, or a CMS plugin is handling structured data automatically.
source_title: Event Structured Data – Event Rich Results on Google

name: Verify File Indexability by Google
summary: Determine whether a specific file type is indexable by Google and ensure proper HTTP headers are set so Google can correctly identify and index the file.
use_when: When publishing non-HTML files (documents, spreadsheets, images, videos, or other formats) to a website and needing to confirm Google can index their content.
avoid_when: When the file type is proprietary, binary, or not in Google's supported list — indexing should not be assumed and alternative content delivery (e.g., HTML transcription) should be considered instead.
source_title: File Types Indexable by Google

name: Implement Flexible Sampling for Paywalled/Subscription Content
summary: Configure a compliant paywall or limited-access content strategy using Google's flexible sampling approach (metering or lead-in) with proper structured data to avoid cloaking penalties.
use_when: A publisher offers subscription-based or paywalled content and needs to remain compliant with Google's policies while limiting free access to content.
avoid_when: Content is fully open-access with no paywall or subscription requirement, or when the publisher intends to serve different content to Googlebot than to users (cloaking).
source_title: Flexible Sampling – Paywalls and Limited Content Access

name: Get Your Website Indexed on Google
summary: A step-by-step process to help Google discover, crawl, and index a new or existing website by taking proactive measures in Google Search Console and optimizing site accessibility.
use_when: When launching a new website, when a site is not appearing in Google search results, or when you want to ensure complete and timely indexing coverage for a site with limited external links.
avoid_when: When the site is intentionally private or not meant for public indexing, or when the site already has strong crawl coverage and established Google presence.
source_title: Get Your Website on Google

name: Book Actions Structured Data Feed Implementation
summary: Build and submit a structured data feed to enable Buy and Borrow book actions in Google Search results, allowing users to purchase or borrow books directly from search.
use_when: When a bookseller or library system wants to enable Google Search as a discovery entry point for buying or borrowing books via Book Actions integration.
avoid_when: When the organization has not yet been accepted as a Google Book Actions partner or has not submitted the required interest form to Google.
source_title: Book Actions Structured Data – Enabling Buy and Borrow Actions

name: Core Update Impact Assessment and Recovery Procedure
summary: A step-by-step process for diagnosing and responding to ranking drops caused by Google core updates, from confirming the update to making content improvements.
use_when: A site experiences a ranking or traffic drop that correlates in timing with a known Google core update announcement.
avoid_when: The ranking drop occurs during a period with no announced core update, or the drop is clearly attributable to a technical issue, manual action, or penalty unrelated to content quality.
source_title: Core Updates – What They Are and How to Respond

name: Developer SEO Technical Implementation Checklist
summary: A structured procedure for developers to ensure their web application is technically optimized for Google Search crawling and indexing.
use_when: Building or auditing a website or web application that needs to perform well in Google Search, especially JavaScript-heavy or single-page applications.
avoid_when: When working on static HTML sites with no JavaScript rendering concerns and already-verified mobile parity — the checklist may be redundant.
source_title: Developer's Guide to Search

name: Implement ClaimReview Structured Data for Fact-Checking Pages
summary: Add ClaimReview structured data to fact-checking article pages to enable summarised fact-check displays in Google Search results.
use_when: A web page reviews or fact-checks a claim made by others and the site meets Google's eligibility requirements for fact-check markup.
avoid_when: The site represents a political entity (campaign, party, or elected official), lacks a corrections policy, or has a mismatch between structured data and page content.
source_title: Fact Check Structured Data – ClaimReview for Fact Checking Articles

name: Fix Canonicalization Issues in Google Search
summary: Diagnose and resolve canonicalization issues where Google selects a different canonical URL than the site owner intends, by identifying the root cause and applying the appropriate fix.
use_when: Google's selected canonical URL differs from the preferred canonical URL shown in the URL Inspection tool in Search Console, or unexpected URLs are being indexed.
avoid_when: The canonical URL Google selects matches the site owner's preferred canonical and there are no indexing anomalies.
source_title: Fix Canonicalization Issues

name: Optimize Content for Google Discover Eligibility and Visibility
summary: A set of best practices site owners should follow to increase the likelihood of their indexed content appearing in Google Discover feeds.
use_when: When creating or auditing content intended to appear in Google Discover, particularly articles and videos targeting interest-based audiences.
avoid_when: When the content type is a job application, petition, form, code repository, or satirical piece without context, as these are unlikely to be recommended by Discover.
source_title: Google Discover – How Content Is Selected and Best Practices

name: Build and Submit a Sitemap to Google
summary: Create a properly formatted sitemap file and submit it to Google so Googlebot can discover and crawl your site's URLs.
use_when: When launching a new site, adding significant new content, or ensuring Google can discover all canonical URLs on a site.
avoid_when: When the site has very few pages that are already well-linked and easily crawlable without a sitemap.
source_title: Build and Submit a Sitemap to Google

name: Add a Favicon Eligible for Google Search Results
summary: Step-by-step process to implement a favicon that meets Google's requirements and can appear in Google Search results.
use_when: When setting up or updating a website favicon to ensure it is eligible to appear in Google Search results.
avoid_when: When the site operates at a subdirectory level (e.g., example.com/brand/) rather than hostname or subdomain level, as Google only supports one favicon per hostname.
source_title: Favicons in Google Search – Guidelines and Requirements

name: Opt Out of Featured Snippets in Google Search
summary: A procedure for site owners to remove or limit their pages from appearing as featured snippets in Google Search results, using meta rules and HTML attributes.
use_when: A site owner wants to prevent specific pages from appearing as featured snippets, either completely or while retaining regular snippet visibility.
avoid_when: The site owner wants to appear as a featured snippet — this playbook only applies to opting out, not opting in, and Google does not support opting in manually.
source_title: Featured Snippets – How They Work and How to Opt Out

name: Diagnose and Fix JavaScript Issues Blocking Google Search Indexing
summary: A process for identifying and resolving JavaScript problems that prevent content or pages from appearing in Google Search, accounting for Web Rendering Service (WRS) limitations.
use_when: Pages are not appearing in Google Search, content is missing from indexed pages, or you suspect JavaScript rendering issues are causing indexing failures in a JavaScript-heavy or single-page application site.
avoid_when: The site has no client-side JavaScript or all content is server-side rendered with no SPA frameworks involved.
source_title: Fix Search Related JavaScript Problems

name: Fix Lazy-Loaded Content for Google Search Crawlability
summary: Implement lazy-loading correctly so Google can discover and index all page content, including images, iframes, and paginated or infinite-scroll content.
use_when: When a site uses lazy-loading for images, iframes, or content sections and there is concern that Googlebot may not be indexing all visible content due to the implementation requiring user interaction (scroll/click) to trigger loading.
avoid_when: When content is designed to be immediately visible above the fold upon page load — lazy-loading should not be applied to above-the-fold content regardless of indexing concerns.
source_title: Fix Lazy Loaded Content for Google Search

name: Google Safe Browsing Repeat Offender Recovery Procedure
summary: A structured process for site owners to remediate their site after being classified as a Google Safe Browsing Repeat Offender and prepare for a successful review request after the 30-day waiting period.
use_when: A site has been classified as a Google Safe Browsing Repeat Offender, the owner has received notification via Search Console email, and review requests are temporarily blocked for 30 days.
avoid_when: The site has only received a standard (non-repeat) Safe Browsing warning or has not yet exhausted review request options in Search Console.
source_title: Google Safe Browsing Repeat Offenders Policy

name: Sitemap Index File Implementation for Large Sites
summary: How to set up and submit a sitemap index file when a site's sitemap exceeds size or URL limits, managing multiple sitemaps through a single index file.
use_when: A sitemap exceeds 50MB uncompressed or 50,000 URLs and must be split into multiple smaller sitemaps that need to be managed and submitted together.
avoid_when: The site's sitemap is within the 50MB and 50,000 URL limits and does not require splitting into multiple files.
source_title: Sitemap Index Files – Managing Multiple Sitemaps

name: Use Google Trends to Develop and Refine an SEO Content Strategy
summary: A workflow for using Google Trends tools to identify content opportunities, seasonal patterns, brand perception, and international expansion signals to inform a site's content and publishing strategy.
use_when: When a site owner or SEO strategist needs to plan content calendars, identify rising topics in their industry, monitor brand search behavior, or explore international audience opportunities.
avoid_when: When used as the sole determinant of what content to create — Google Trends should complement, not replace, alignment with overall business goals and user interests.
source_title: Get Started with Google Trends for SEO

name: Implement Structured Data with JavaScript
summary: A best-practice procedure for generating and validating structured data using JavaScript (via Google Tag Manager or custom JS), including caching safeguards for Googlebot.
use_when: When building or updating a website that uses JavaScript to dynamically generate structured data for Google Search, including Product, Article, or other schema markup.
avoid_when: When structured data can be statically embedded in server-rendered HTML without JavaScript, or when the content changes frequently (e.g., real-time product pricing/availability) and server-side rendering is feasible — in those cases prefer server-side inclusion to avoid unreliable crawls.
source_title: Generate Structured Data with JavaScript

name: Verify Googlebot Authenticity via Reverse DNS Lookup
summary: A procedure for site owners to confirm that a crawl request genuinely originates from Googlebot rather than a spoofed crawler, before taking any blocking action.
use_when: When a site owner suspects malicious or spoofed crawler activity appearing to be Googlebot in server logs, or before deciding to block any request claiming to be Googlebot.
avoid_when: When Googlebot access is already confirmed via Google Search Console or other trusted verification methods, or when blanket crawler blocking is not under consideration.
source_title: Googlebot – What It Is and How It Works

name: Explicit Content Site Configuration for Google Search
summary: Configure an adult/explicit content site so Google can accurately categorize it, ensure proper SafeSearch filtering, and avoid ranking penalties from inaccessible content.
use_when: A site hosts explicit or adult content and needs to ensure Google correctly identifies, categorizes, and appropriately filters it in search results.
avoid_when: The site does not host explicit content, or the content violates Google's policies (e.g., CSAM) — no configuration will make such content eligible.
source_title: Guidelines for Sites with Explicit Content

name: AI-Assisted Content Quality Assurance Playbook
summary: A review and publishing procedure for site owners using AI tools to create content, ensuring it meets Google's quality and helpfulness standards.
use_when: When a site owner or content team uses AI tools to generate or assist in creating content intended for publication and search indexing.
avoid_when: When content is entirely human-written without any AI assistance, making this review layer unnecessary for AI-specific concerns.
source_title: Guidance on Generative AI Content

name: Configure Locale-Adaptive Pages for Full Google Indexing
summary: Restructure locale-adaptive pages so all locale variants are discoverable and indexable by Googlebot, which crawls primarily from US-based IPs without Accept-Language headers.
use_when: A site serves different content at a single URL based on the visitor's detected country or language preference, and not all locale variants are appearing in Google search results.
avoid_when: The site already uses separate URLs per locale with proper hreflang annotations and all variants are being indexed correctly.
source_title: How Google Crawls Locale Adaptive Pages

name: Implementing Structured Data for Rich Results
summary: A procedure for correctly adding structured data to web pages to enable rich results in Google Search, covering format selection, placement rules, property requirements, and validation.
use_when: When adding structured data markup to web pages to qualify for Google rich results and improve search appearance and click-through rates.
avoid_when: When the page is empty or created solely to hold structured data, or when the information described is not visible to the user on the page.
source_title: How Structured Data Works – Introduction to Rich Results

name: Remove Images from Google Search Results
summary: A procedure for removing specific images or groups of images from Google Search and Google Images using either robots.txt rules or noindex HTTP response headers.
use_when: When you need to remove one or more images from Google Search results or Google Images, either for a specific URL, a pattern of URLs, or images embedded on specific pages.
avoid_when: When you need an immediate removal — use Google's Removals tool instead for faster results. Also avoid this playbook if you only want to remove images from Google specifically without affecting other search engines (robots.txt blocks all search engines).
source_title: Image Removals – Removing Images from Google Search Results

name: Diagnose and Resolve HTTP Status Code Issues Affecting Google Indexing
summary: A reference-based procedure for identifying what each HTTP status code means for Googlebot crawling and indexing, and how to interpret Search Console Page Indexing report errors to take corrective action.
use_when: When a site owner or SEO practitioner notices indexing issues, unexpected URL removals, or crawl errors in Google Search Console's Page Indexing report and needs to map status codes to their indexing impact.
avoid_when: When the site is returning 200 OK responses with valid content and no indexing errors are present in Search Console — no corrective action is needed.
source_title: HTTP Status Codes, Network Errors, and DNS Errors

name: Add Image License Metadata for Google Images Licensable Badge
summary: Implement image licensing metadata using structured data (schema.org) and/or IPTC photo metadata to make images eligible for the Licensable badge in Google Images.
use_when: When publishing images on web pages that have licensing terms and you want to surface licensing information in Google Images search results.
avoid_when: When images are behind a login or account wall, or when pages are blocked by robots.txt or robots meta tags — Google cannot discover the metadata in these cases.
source_title: Image License Metadata – Licensable Badge in Google Images

name: Image SEO Implementation Playbook for Google Search
summary: A structured process for optimizing images on web pages to ensure they are discoverable, indexable, and well-ranked in Google Search and Google Images.
use_when: When adding or auditing images on a website to maximize visibility in Google Search, Google Images, or Discover.
avoid_when: When images are intentionally excluded from search indexing or when using images purely for decorative CSS purposes that should not appear in search results.
source_title: Image SEO Best Practices for Google Search

name: Implement an Image Sitemap to Help Google Discover Site Images
summary: Create and configure an image sitemap so Google can discover images on your site, including those loaded via JavaScript that crawlers might otherwise miss.
use_when: When your site contains images that Google may not discover through standard crawling, particularly images loaded via JavaScript, or when you want to ensure comprehensive image indexing.
avoid_when: When your site has no images, or when all images are already easily discoverable through standard HTML and you have no indexing issues with image content.
source_title: Image Sitemaps – Helping Google Find Site Images

name: Preventing a Web Page from Appearing in Google Search Results
summary: A step-by-step approach to properly block a URL from being indexed and appearing in Google Search, using the correct mechanisms instead of relying solely on robots.txt.
use_when: When the goal is to completely prevent a specific page or resource from appearing in Google Search results, especially when the page may be linked to from external sites.
avoid_when: When the goal is only to reduce crawler traffic load on the server without needing to prevent indexing — in that case, robots.txt alone is sufficient.
source_title: Introduction to robots.txt

name: Build a Search Console Bubble Chart in Looker Studio for SEO Analysis
summary: Create a multi-dimensional bubble chart in Looker Studio using Google Search Console data to visualize query performance across position, CTR, clicks, and device category simultaneously.
use_when: A site has many long-tail queries that are difficult to analyze in tabular form, and you need to identify which queries to prioritize for SEO improvement.
avoid_when: The site has very few queries or limited Search Console data, making quadrant-based analysis statistically unreliable.
source_title: Improve SEO with a Search Console Bubble Chart in Looker Studio

name: Avoid Intrusive Interstitials and Use Compliant Overlay Alternatives
summary: A procedure for site owners to replace intrusive interstitials and dialogs with compliant, non-obstructive alternatives that preserve search engine indexability and user content access.
use_when: When a website uses full-page interstitials, pop-up overlays, or redirects users to separate consent/input pages for promotional or non-mandatory purposes.
avoid_when: When the interstitial is legally mandatory (e.g., age verification for casino or adult content sites), in which case different rules apply — though even then, content must remain indexable beneath the overlay.
source_title: Interstitials and Dialogs – Avoiding Intrusive Page Overlays

name: JavaScript SEO Implementation Checklist for Google Crawlability
summary: A structured procedure to ensure JavaScript-rendered web pages are properly crawled, rendered, and indexed by Google, covering links, status codes, robots directives, content visibility, canonicals, and structured data.
use_when: When building or auditing a JavaScript-heavy website (SPA, app shell model, or client-side rendered app) that needs to be discoverable and indexable by Google Search.
avoid_when: When the site is fully server-side rendered with no JavaScript-dependent content, as most steps are unnecessary for static or traditional server-rendered pages.
source_title: JavaScript SEO Basics – How Google Crawls and Renders JavaScript

name: Redact and Remove Sensitive Information from Google Search
summary: A step-by-step procedure for properly redacting sensitive information from documents and images before publishing, and for removing already-indexed content from Google Search if sensitive information was accidentally published.
use_when: When preparing documents or images containing sensitive information for web publication, or when sensitive/redacted information has already been published and indexed by Google Search.
avoid_when: When the content does not contain any sensitive or non-public information, or when the document is not intended for public web publication.
source_title: Keep Redacted Information Out of Google Search

name: International & Multilingual Site Optimisation Setup
summary: A structured approach for site owners to optimise Google Search results when serving content in multiple languages or targeting multiple geographic regions.
use_when: A site serves content in more than one language, targets users in multiple countries, or both — and the owner wants Google to correctly match locale-specific pages to the right searchers.
avoid_when: The site serves a single language to a single geographic market with no plans for international or multilingual expansion.
source_title: International and Multilingual Sites – Overview

name: Crawlable Link and Anchor Text Best Practices Implementation
summary: A procedure for ensuring links on a site are crawlable by Google and that anchor text meets quality standards for both users and search engines.
use_when: When auditing or building a website's internal and external link structure to ensure Google can discover and understand all important pages via links.
avoid_when: When links are intentionally set to nofollow for all external sources as a blanket policy — this playbook requires nuanced rel attribute application.
source_title: Link Best Practices – Crawlability and Anchor Text

name: Implement MemberProgram Loyalty Structured Data
summary: Add MemberProgram structured data to a site so Google Search can display loyalty benefits (special prices, points) with products and knowledge panels in search results.
use_when: A merchant has a loyalty programme with membership tiers and wants to surface loyalty benefits (prices, points) in Google Search results for supported countries (AU, BR, CA, FR, DE, MX, UK, US).
avoid_when: Merchant Center loyalty programme configurations are already in place and will take precedence; do not implement if the site targets countries outside the supported list, as the data will not surface in results.
source_title: Loyalty Program Structured Data – MemberProgram for Membership Benefits

name: Remediate Malware or Unwanted Software Detected by Google
summary: Step-by-step process for identifying, addressing, and requesting review for malware or unwanted software issues flagged by Google on a website or application.
use_when: Google has flagged your site in the Security Issues report in Search Console for hosting malware, harmful downloads, or unwanted software that violates Google's guidelines.
avoid_when: The site has not received any Security Issues report notifications and is not suspected of hosting malicious or deceptive software.
source_title: Malware and Unwanted Software – What They Are and Google's Guidelines

name: Managing Crawling of Faceted Navigation URLs
summary: A set of practices to control how crawlers handle faceted navigation URLs, either by blocking them entirely or minimising their negative crawling impact when indexing is needed.
use_when: A website uses faceted navigation (e.g., product filters, article filters) that generates large numbers of parameter-based URLs, causing overcrawling or slow discovery of useful pages.
avoid_when: The site does not use faceted navigation or URL parameters, or when faceted URLs are already canonicalised and crawl budget is not a concern.
source_title: Managing Crawling of Faceted Navigation URLs

name: Implement Merchant Listing Structured Data for Product Pages
summary: A procedure for correctly implementing Product structured data on merchant listing pages to qualify for Google's merchant listing rich results in search.
use_when: You are adding or auditing structured data on pages where shoppers can directly purchase a product, including single-product pages or pages with product variants.
avoid_when: The page is a category page listing many products, a page with links to other sites selling the product, or the product falls under prohibited categories (firearms, recreational drugs, tobacco/vaping, gambling-related products).
source_title: Merchant Listing Structured Data – Products for Sale

name: Implement ShippingService Structured Data for Google Search
summary: Add ShippingService structured data to a site so Google can display shipping costs and delivery times in search results and knowledge panels.
use_when: When a merchant wants to surface shipping policy information (costs, delivery times, regions) in Google Search results alongside products.
avoid_when: When the site has no stable or structured shipping policy, or when product-level shipping variations are not yet defined and could cause inconsistent markup.
source_title: Merchant Shipping Policy Structured Data – ShippingService

name: Ongoing SEO Maintenance Workflow
summary: A repeatable maintenance procedure for sustaining SEO performance over time, covering monitoring, content upkeep, technical health, and link profile management.
use_when: A website is already optimised and requires ongoing maintenance to sustain search performance, catch regressions early, and adapt to algorithm updates or competitor changes.
avoid_when: A site is being built or optimised for the first time and has not yet established baseline SEO performance to monitor.
source_title: Maintaining Your Website's SEO

name: Crawl Budget Optimisation for Large Sites
summary: A set of best practices for large site owners to maximise crawling efficiency and ensure Google crawls the most valuable pages.
use_when: Managing a very large site with many pages that change frequently, where crawl budget is a limiting factor for indexing important content.
avoid_when: Running a small or medium site where crawl budget is not a concern, or where all pages are already being crawled and indexed efficiently.
source_title: Large Site Owner's Guide to Managing Your Crawl Budget

name: Implement MerchantReturnPolicy Structured Data
summary: Add MerchantReturnPolicy structured data to a site so Google can display return policy information alongside products and in knowledge panels in search results.
use_when: When a merchant wants Google Search to display return policy details (conditions, methods, fees, refund options) in search results alongside products or in knowledge panels.
avoid_when: When return policy details are frequently changing or complex — in that case, configuring return policies in Google Merchant Center or Search Console account-level settings is more appropriate. Also avoid if Search Console return policy settings are already configured, as they will override structured data.
source_title: Merchant Return Policy Structured Data – MerchantReturnPolicy

name: Site Migration with URL Changes Playbook
summary: A structured procedure for moving a site that involves URL changes (domain, protocol, subdomain, or directory), ensuring minimal ranking loss and proper indexing by Google.
use_when: When migrating a site that involves changes to URLs, including domain name changes, HTTP to HTTPS upgrades, subdomain changes, or directory restructuring.
avoid_when: When only making content changes without URL structure changes, or when performing a purely cosmetic redesign with no URL modifications.
source_title: Move a Site with URL Changes – Migrations and Domain Changes

name: Mobile-First Indexing Compliance Checklist
summary: Ensure a website meets Google's mobile-first indexing requirements so the mobile version is correctly crawled, indexed, and ranked.
use_when: When auditing an existing site for mobile-first indexing compliance, launching a new website, or migrating to a mobile-friendly configuration.
avoid_when: When the site has already been verified as fully mobile-first compliant and no structural or content changes have been made.
source_title: Mobile First Indexing Best Practices

name: Implement Google-Supported Meta Tags and HTML Attributes
summary: A procedure for correctly adding meta tags and HTML attributes that Google recognizes, while avoiding unsupported or ineffective tags that will be ignored.
use_when: When setting up or auditing the head section of HTML pages to ensure proper communication of page metadata to Google's crawler.
avoid_when: When working with non-HTML content types or when the head section is dynamically injected via JavaScript (which should be avoided per Google's guidelines).
source_title: Meta Tags and HTML Attributes that Google Supports

name: Implement Organisation Structured Data on Homepage
summary: Add Organisation schema markup to a website's homepage to help Google disambiguate the business entity and improve search result presentation including logo and knowledge panel.
use_when: When setting up or auditing structured data for a business website that lacks Organisation schema markup on its homepage, or when Google is misidentifying the organisation in search results.
avoid_when: When the entity is a person rather than an organisation (use Person schema instead), or when a more specific subtype like LocalBusiness, Corporation, or EducationalOrganisation is clearly applicable without a general Organisation wrapper.
source_title: Organisation Structured Data – Helping Google Understand Your Business Entity

name: Implement Movie Carousel Structured Data for Mobile Rich Results
summary: Mark up a movie list summary page and individual movie detail pages with ItemList and Movie schema.org structured data to enable movie carousel rich results on Google mobile search.
use_when: You have a website listing multiple movies with individual detail pages and want to enable movie carousel rich results on Google mobile search.
avoid_when: Your site only has a single movie page without a summary list page, or you are targeting desktop-only experiences (carousel is mobile-only).
source_title: Movie Carousel Structured Data – Movie Rich Results on Mobile

name: Create and Maintain a Google News Sitemap
summary: Step-by-step procedure for news publishers to create, configure, and maintain a news sitemap that Google News can crawl to discover and index news articles.
use_when: When operating a news publishing site and wanting to ensure Google News discovers and indexes articles promptly after publication.
avoid_when: When articles are older than two days, as those URLs should not be included in the news sitemap.
source_title: News Sitemaps – Submitting News Articles to Google

name: Remove a Page from Google Search Results
summary: A step-by-step procedure for site owners to remove a page from Google Search, covering both quick temporary removal and permanent blocking methods.
use_when: A site owner needs to remove a specific page from Google Search results, either urgently or permanently, due to outdated content, sensitive information, or access control requirements.
avoid_when: The goal is to block a page from all search engines simultaneously without relying on noindex support, or when the page should remain accessible to users but only be delisted from Google temporarily.
source_title: Page Removals – How to Remove a Page from Google Search

name: Package Tracking Early Adopters Program: API Integration for Delivery Companies
summary: How a delivery company should evaluate eligibility, prepare their API, and apply to participate in Google's Package Tracking Early Adopters Program.
use_when: A delivery company is based in India, Japan, or Brazil, or is the sole authorized provider of tracking data for a company servicing those areas, and wants to surface package tracking in Google Search.
avoid_when: The delivery company does not operate in or service India, Japan, or Brazil, or cannot meet the API response time and data privacy requirements.
source_title: Package Tracking – Early Adopters Program for Delivery Companies

name: Ecommerce Pagination SEO Implementation Playbook
summary: Implement crawlable, indexable pagination for ecommerce sites using traditional pagination, Load More buttons, or infinite scroll so Google can discover all content.
use_when: Building or auditing an ecommerce site that displays products or content across multiple pages using pagination, infinite scroll, or Load More patterns.
avoid_when: Site displays all content on a single page without any pagination or incremental loading mechanism.
source_title: Pagination and Incremental Page Loading for Ecommerce

name: Implement Signed Exchanges (SXG) for AMP Pages
summary: Enable Signed Exchanges on AMP pages using the AMP-specific guide and monitor errors via the AMP status report in Search Console.
use_when: When a site serves AMP pages and wants to enable privacy-preserving prefetching via Google Search using Signed Exchanges.
avoid_when: When the site does not use AMP (use the non-AMP SXG guide instead), or when AMP implementation is not already in place and stable.
source_title: Signed Exchanges – Prefetching for Better Page Experience

name: Implement MusicRecording Schema Markup
summary: Add structured data markup for a specific recorded track or song using Schema.org MusicRecording type to enable Google Rich Results, music metadata display, and knowledge panel integration.
use_when: Marking up a specific recorded song or track on a web page to enable rich results in Google Search, Google Assistant music answers, or knowledge panel integration for music content.
avoid_when: Marking up an entire album (use MusicAlbum), an unrecorded musical composition or sheet music (use MusicComposition), or a generic playlist without individual track focus (use MusicPlaylist).
source_title: MusicRecording

name: Implement NewsArticle Schema Markup for News Publishers
summary: Step-by-step procedure to add valid NewsArticle structured data to news content for Google Top Stories, AMP rich results, and News tab eligibility.
use_when: Publishing timely, reported journalism content and seeking eligibility for Google Top Stories carousel, News tab appearances, or AMP-powered news rich results.
avoid_when: Content is promotional, advertorial, sponsored, or satirical — use AdvertiserContentArticle or SatiricalArticle subtypes instead.
source_title: NewsArticle

name: Malware Prevention Security Checklist for Site Owners
summary: A recurring security hygiene procedure for website owners to prevent malware infections, detect unauthorized changes, and respond to security issues before they escalate.
use_when: When setting up ongoing security monitoring for a website, after launching a new site, or when hardening an existing site against hacking and malware risks.
avoid_when: When a site has already been confirmed hacked — use Google's Help for Hacked Sites recovery process instead of this prevention checklist.
source_title: Prevent a Malware Infection – Security Best Practices for Site Owners

name: Implement ProductGroup Structured Data for Product Variants
summary: Add ProductGroup and Product schema markup to enable Google to understand product variants (size, color, material, etc.) and display them in merchant listing experiences.
use_when: You have products sold in multiple variations (size, color, material, pattern) and want them eligible for variant display in Google merchant listing experiences.
avoid_when: Products have no meaningful variants or all variants share the same URL without distinguishable parameters.
source_title: Product Variants Structured Data – ProductGroup for Size and Color Variants

name: Implement ProfilePage Structured Data for Creator Profiles
summary: Add ProfilePage schema markup to creator/author profile pages to help Google understand site contributors and improve appearance in search results including Discussions and Forums features.
use_when: A site has dedicated creator, author, or user profile pages featuring a single person or organisation, such as author bio pages on news sites, forum user profiles, or creator pages on content platforms.
avoid_when: The page features multiple people or organisations as its primary focus, or the page is not dedicated to a single creator's identity/bio but merely lists content they produced.
source_title: Profile Page Structured Data – ProfilePage for Creator Profiles

name: Implement Recipe Structured Data for Rich Results
summary: Mark up recipe content with schema.org Recipe structured data to enable rich results in Google Search, including recipe cards, carousels, and filters.
use_when: When publishing web content about preparing a specific dish and wanting to be eligible for recipe rich results in Google Search and Google Images.
avoid_when: When content is not about preparing a dish (e.g., facial scrub instructions, party planning ideas, or non-food how-to content) — Recipe markup is invalid for such use cases.
source_title: Recipe Structured Data – Rich Results for Recipes

name: Implement QAPage Structured Data for Question & Answer Pages
summary: Add schema.org QAPage markup to pages where users can submit answers to a single question, enabling rich results in Google Search.
use_when: A web page contains a single question followed by multiple user-submitted answers, such as a community Q&A or forum thread format.
avoid_when: The page has a single definitive answer provided by the site with no user-submitted alternatives (use FAQPage markup instead), or the page contains multiple questions.
source_title: Q&A Page Structured Data – QAPage for Questions and Answers

name: Qualify Outbound Links with rel Attributes for Google
summary: Apply appropriate rel attribute values to outbound anchor elements to communicate the nature of linked relationships to Google, covering paid, user-generated, and non-endorsed links.
use_when: When a site contains outbound links that are paid/sponsored, inserted by users (e.g., comments, forums), or links the site owner does not endorse or want Google to follow.
avoid_when: When links are normal editorial links that Google should follow unconditionally — no rel attribute is needed in that case. Also avoid using nofollow as a substitute for noindex when the goal is to prevent a page from being indexed.
source_title: rel Attributes – Qualifying Outbound Links for Google

name: Implementing URL Redirects for Google Search
summary: How to correctly implement URL redirects so that Google Search properly interprets canonicalization signals and shows the intended URL in search results.
use_when: When a site moves to a new domain, a page is removed or relocated, multiple URLs resolve to the same content, or two websites are being merged.
avoid_when: When content is temporarily unavailable and you want Google to continue showing the original URL in search results (use temporary redirects instead of permanent ones).
source_title: Redirects and Google Search – Types, Implementation, and Canonicalization

name: Implement Structured Data for Rich Results in Google Search
summary: Add structured data markup to web pages so Google can understand page content and display rich results features in search.
use_when: A site owner wants to enhance how their pages appear in Google Search results by enabling rich result features such as recipe cards, job postings, events, reviews, breadcrumbs, or other supported content types.
avoid_when: The page content does not match any of the supported structured data types, or the site is in early development and not yet indexed by Google.
source_title: Ranking and Search Appearance – Overview

name: Share Product Data with Google via Structured Data and Merchant Center
summary: A dual-channel approach to maximizing product visibility on Google surfaces by implementing structured data on product pages and uploading product feeds to Google Merchant Center.
use_when: When setting up or improving ecommerce product visibility across Google Search, Google Shopping tab, and other Google surfaces, especially when rich results and product snippets are desired.
avoid_when: When the goal is to prevent page content from being used in Google snippets, or when the site is not ecommerce-focused and has no product data to share.
source_title: Share Product Data with Google – Structured Data and Merchant Center

name: Google Search Essentials Compliance Playbook
summary: A foundational procedure for site owners to ensure their web content is eligible for indexing and ranking in Google Search by meeting all three layers of Search Essentials.
use_when: When launching a new website, auditing an existing site for search eligibility, or diagnosing why pages are not appearing in Google Search results.
avoid_when: When the site is intentionally private, behind authentication, or not intended to be indexed by search engines.
source_title: Search Essentials – Overview

name: Google Indexability Technical Requirements Checklist
summary: Ensure a web page meets all five baseline technical requirements for Google to crawl, index, and serve it in search results.
use_when: When auditing a page that is not appearing in Google search results, launching a new page, or conducting a technical SEO review to confirm indexability.
avoid_when: When the page is intentionally kept private or behind a login and is not meant to be indexed by Google.
source_title: Search Essentials – Technical Requirements

name: Implement Robots Meta Tags and X-Robots-Tag Directives
summary: Configure page-level and element-level indexing instructions for Google and other search engines using robots meta tags, X-Robots-Tag HTTP headers, and data-nosnippet attributes.
use_when: When you need to control how Google indexes pages, generates snippets, shows cached versions, follows links, or displays image/video previews — including for non-HTML files like PDFs or videos.
avoid_when: When the goal is to block crawling entirely (use robots.txt instead), or when JavaScript is being used to dynamically add/remove data-nosnippet attributes from existing DOM nodes.
source_title: Robots Meta Tag, data nosnippet, and X Robots Tag Specifications

name: Implement Signed Exchanges (SXG) for Non-AMP Pages
summary: Enable Signed Exchanges on non-AMP pages to allow Google Search to prefetch key resources, improving LCP and overall page experience scores.
use_when: When a site serves non-AMP pages and wants to improve Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) and Core Web Vitals by enabling Google Search prefetching via Signed Exchanges.
avoid_when: When the site serves AMP pages (a separate AMP-specific guide should be used instead), or when the site lacks the server-side infrastructure to generate and serve SXG responses.
source_title: Signed Exchanges – Prefetching for Better Page Experience

name: SEO Starter Guide: Core Website Optimisation Procedure
summary: A foundational procedure for optimising a website so Google can discover, understand, and rank its content, while also serving users well.
use_when: When launching a new website, auditing an existing site for basic SEO compliance, or onboarding a site owner who needs foundational SEO implementation.
avoid_when: When seeking advanced technical SEO fixes or when the site already has a mature SEO foundation and requires specialist optimisation beyond basics.
source_title: SEO Starter Guide

name: Meta Description Optimization for Google Snippets
summary: A set of best practices for writing and configuring meta descriptions to improve snippet quality and search traffic in Google Search results.
use_when: When creating or auditing meta descriptions for web pages intended to rank in Google Search, especially when snippet quality or click-through rates need improvement.
avoid_when: When the site intentionally suppresses snippets using nosnippet tags, or when pages are excluded from Google indexing entirely.
source_title: Snippets and Meta Descriptions

name: Sitemap Need Assessment and Implementation Decision
summary: Evaluate whether a sitemap is needed for a site based on size, link structure, content type, and age, then determine if CMS auto-generation covers the requirement or manual creation is needed.
use_when: When launching a new site, auditing an existing site's crawlability, or diagnosing indexation issues related to pages being overlooked by search engines.
avoid_when: When the site has fewer than 500 indexable pages, is comprehensively internally linked, and has no rich media or news content requiring enhanced metadata.
source_title: Sitemaps – What They Are and When to Use One

name: Google Spam Policy Compliance Audit
summary: Audit a website to identify and remediate practices that violate Google's spam policies, including cloaking, doorway pages, hidden text, keyword stuffing, and link spam.
use_when: When conducting an SEO audit, recovering from a Google penalty, reviewing a site before launch, or ensuring ongoing compliance with Google's spam policies.
avoid_when: When the site has already been reviewed and confirmed compliant within the last audit cycle and no new content or link building has occurred since.
source_title: Spam Policies – Cloaking Through Link Spam

name: Implement Product Schema Markup for E-Commerce Pages
summary: Add valid Schema.org Product structured data to individual product pages to enable Google Rich Results with price, availability, and review ratings.
use_when: Marking up an individual product page (physical good, digital product, service, ticket, rental) for search engine visibility and shopping graph inclusion.
avoid_when: The page is a category listing or displays multiple products — use a different schema type instead. Also avoid when variants (size, color) need to be grouped — use ProductGroup with isVariantOf instead.
source_title: Product

name: Temporarily Disable a Website While Minimising SEO Impact
summary: A structured approach for taking a website partially or fully offline while preserving its Google Search presence and minimising recovery time.
use_when: A business temporarily cannot fulfil orders, has many products out of stock, or must urgently take its website offline for a short period.
avoid_when: The site is being permanently shut down or the downtime is expected to extend beyond a few days without a placeholder page strategy in place.
source_title: Temporarily Pause or Disable a Website

name: Implement SoftwareApplication Structured Data for Rich Results
summary: Add schema.org SoftwareApplication markup to software app pages to enable rich results in Google Search showing ratings, price, and OS compatibility.
use_when: You have a software application page where users can download or use an app and want to enable rich result features in Google Search.
avoid_when: The app is not actually downloadable or usable by users, or the structured data would not reflect what is visible and accessible on the page.
source_title: Software App Structured Data – SoftwareApplication Rich Results

name: Identify and Remediate Social Engineering / Phishing Content on a Website
summary: A procedure for detecting, classifying, and addressing social engineering content (phishing, deceptive content, unlabelled third-party services, embedded malicious resources) on a website to avoid Google's 'Deceptive site ahead' warning in Chrome.
use_when: A site has received a 'Deceptive site ahead' warning in Chrome, is suspected of hosting phishing or deceptive content, contains third-party ads or embedded resources that may be flagging social engineering signals, or has potentially been compromised by hackers injecting deceptive pages.
avoid_when: The site has already been fully audited and cleared by Google Search Console with no active security issues flagged; no user-facing warnings are present and no third-party components are in use.
source_title: Social Engineering – Phishing and Deceptive Sites

name: Implement Speakable Structured Data for News Content
summary: Add speakable schema.org markup to news articles so Google Assistant can identify and read aloud key sections on smart speaker devices.
use_when: Publishing news articles intended to appear in Google Assistant audio responses on smart speaker devices, especially for topical news queries.
avoid_when: Content includes datelines, photo captions, or source attributions that would sound confusing in voice-only playback; also avoid when the content is not news-oriented or not targeting voice search.
source_title: Speakable Structured Data – Audio Playback for News Content

name: Write High-Quality Ecommerce Product Reviews for SEO
summary: A structured process for creating in-depth, first-hand product reviews that satisfy Google's review quality standards and improve search visibility.
use_when: Creating or auditing product reviews for ecommerce sites, editorial review pages, or aggregate rating content intended to rank in Google Search.
avoid_when: Writing purely informational product descriptions or manufacturer spec summaries without direct product experience, as this approach is likely to underperform in Google's reviews system.
source_title: Write High Quality Reviews for Ecommerce

name: Opt Out of Google Translated Results
summary: Procedure for site owners to prevent Google from translating their pages in search results and to monitor translated result performance via Search Console.
use_when: When a site owner wants to prevent Google from machine-translating their content in search results, or when ad networks on the site are not displaying correctly after users click translated results.
avoid_when: When the site owner wants to reach a broader multilingual audience and benefits from Google's translated results feature.
source_title: Translated Results – Cross Language Search Results

name: Page Experience Optimization Checklist for Google Search
summary: A structured assessment procedure for site owners to evaluate and improve page experience signals recognized by Google's ranking systems, covering Core Web Vitals and broader UX factors.
use_when: When auditing or optimizing a website to improve Google Search performance related to page experience signals, especially when Core Web Vitals scores are being reviewed or UX quality is in question.
avoid_when: When seeking a guaranteed ranking improvement strategy — page experience improvements do not guarantee top rankings and should not replace content quality efforts.
source_title: Understanding Page Experience in Google Search

name: Structured Data Deployment Workflow
summary: A seven-step process to correctly build, validate, deploy, and monitor structured data for Google Search rich results eligibility.
use_when: Implementing any structured data feature type on a website for the first time, or when updating existing structured data to ensure correct deployment and Google indexing.
avoid_when: Pages are intentionally blocked from Google via robots.txt, noindex tags, or login requirements, as Google cannot crawl or index the structured data regardless of implementation quality.
source_title: Structured Data Deployment Workflow – How to Build, Test, and Release

name: Implement Structured Data for Host Carousel Beta Rich Result
summary: Add ItemList structured data combined with a supported content type to a summary page to qualify for Google's beta carousel rich result, enabling horizontally scrollable entity tiles showing price, ratings, and images.
use_when: When a site has a summary/listing page with multiple entities (products, businesses, etc.) and wants to be eligible for Google's beta structured data carousel rich result in search.
avoid_when: When the site only has individual detail pages with no summary/listing page, or when the content does not map to any supported structured data content types.
source_title: Structured Data Carousels Beta – New Host Carousel Format

name: Implement Structured Data for Ecommerce Pages
summary: A step-by-step procedure for adding and verifying structured data on ecommerce sites to improve Google's understanding of page content and enable rich results in search.
use_when: Setting up or auditing structured data on an ecommerce website, particularly when product pages, business information, or reviews are not generating rich results in Google Search.
avoid_when: The site does not have product pages, physical locations, reviews, or videos that would benefit from structured data markup, or when the ecommerce platform already auto-generates verified structured data.
source_title: Structured Data for Ecommerce Sites – Relevant Schema Types

name: Structured Data Implementation for Rich Results Eligibility
summary: A procedure for correctly implementing structured data on web pages to maximize eligibility and quality for Google rich results in search.
use_when: When adding or auditing structured data markup on web pages intended to appear as rich results in Google Search.
avoid_when: When the page content violates Google's spam or content policies, as structured data will not help and may trigger a manual action.
source_title: Structured Data General Guidelines

name: Implement Structured Data for Subscription/Paywalled Content
summary: Add schema.org structured data markup to paywalled pages so Google can differentiate gated content from cloaking, enabling proper crawling and indexing of subscription-based content.
use_when: A site offers subscription-based or registration-gated content that should still be crawled and indexed by Google, and the paywalled sections need to be clearly signaled to avoid cloaking violations.
avoid_when: Paywalled content should not be indexed at all — in that case, structured data is not needed and should not be added.
source_title: Subscription and Paywalled Content Structured Data

name: Implement hreflang Annotations for Localized Pages
summary: A procedure for telling Google about multiple localized versions of a page using hreflang annotations, so Search can route users to the most appropriate language or regional variant.
use_when: A site has multiple versions of a page for different languages or regions, including when only templates are translated, when content has small regional variations, or when content is fully translated into multiple languages.
avoid_when: The site has only one language/region version of each page and no localization variants exist.
source_title: Tell Google About Localized Versions of Your Pages – hreflang Annotations

name: Title Link Optimization for Google Search
summary: A set of best practices to improve the quality of title links generated by Google for search results, covering what to include, avoid, and expect after changes.
use_when: When auditing or creating page titles to improve how Google displays title links in search results, or when title links are appearing incorrectly or suboptimally in Google Search.
avoid_when: When the page is intentionally excluded from Google Search indexing (e.g., noindex pages) or when title link appearance is controlled by a platform with no access to the title HTML element.
source_title: Title Links in Google Search – How They Are Generated and Best Practices

name: Optimize Top Places List for Google Rich Result Eligibility
summary: Ensure a top places list meets Google's editorial and content criteria to be eligible for display as a Top Places List rich result in business search results.
use_when: A site owner or content publisher has created or is planning to create a curated list of top businesses (e.g., restaurants, hotels) and wants it to be eligible for Google's Top Places List rich result feature.
avoid_when: The business being listed does not have a physical location, or the site owner is attempting to guarantee or directly request inclusion in Top Places List rich results.
source_title: Top Places List – Site Eligibility for Business Mentions

name: Troubleshoot Soft 404 Crawling Errors
summary: Diagnose and resolve soft 404 errors where pages return HTTP 200 but display 'page not found' content, preventing proper indexing by Googlebot.
use_when: A URL is flagged as a soft 404 in Google Search Console, or when a valid page is not being indexed despite appearing to load correctly.
avoid_when: The page is intentionally empty or unavailable and should not be indexed — in that case, return a proper 404 or 410 HTTP status code instead.
source_title: Troubleshoot Google Search Crawling Errors

name: Fix HTML Head Element to Ensure Google Processes Page Metadata
summary: Ensure the HTML head element contains only valid elements so Google can correctly process all page metadata, including meta descriptions, canonical links, robots tags, and structured data.
use_when: When auditing or fixing a page where Google is not processing metadata (meta description, canonical, robots tag, structured data) correctly, or when HTML validation errors are found in the head element.
avoid_when: When the page has no metadata in the head element or when the head element is already fully valid per HTML standard.
source_title: Use Valid HTML to Specify Page Metadata

name: Implement VacationRental Structured Data for Google Search Rich Results
summary: Add schema.org VacationRental structured data markup to vacation rental property pages to make them eligible for enhanced search result listings on Google.
use_when: You manage vacation rental property pages and want to enable rich results in Google Search, including enhanced listing details.
avoid_when: Pages are blocked by robots.txt, noindex tags, or login requirements — these are ineligible for rich results regardless of markup.
source_title: Vacation Rental Structured Data – VacationRental Listings

name: Video SEO Setup for Google Search Eligibility
summary: A step-by-step procedure to make videos eligible for Google Search video features including main search results, Video mode, Key Moments, and Live Badge.
use_when: When publishing videos on a website and wanting them to appear in Google Search video features, Video mode, Key Moments, or other rich video formats.
avoid_when: When videos are intended to be private, gated behind login, or blocked from public access by design.
source_title: Video SEO Best Practices for Google Search

name: Resolve Incorrect SafeSearch Explicit Flagging
summary: A step-by-step process to diagnose and correct a site that has been incorrectly flagged as explicit by Google's SafeSearch systems, covering nuanced or suggestive content like lingerie, massage, or sex education sites.
use_when: A site that contains non-explicitly sexual content (e.g., lingerie, massage, sex education) is being filtered or suppressed in Google SafeSearch results, or is missing rich results, featured snippets, or video previews.
avoid_when: The site genuinely contains significant nudity, sexually explicit content (including CGI), or graphic violence — in which case the explicit classification is likely correct and this process will not result in an overturn.
source_title: What to Do If Your Site Is Incorrectly Flagged as Explicit

name: Optimise Website for Google Search Visual Elements
summary: A reference-based procedure for site owners to identify which Google Search result visual elements (text results, rich results, image results, video results) apply to their site and optimise accordingly.
use_when: When a site owner wants to improve how their website appears in Google Search results by targeting specific visual elements such as rich results, attribution, title links, or snippets.
avoid_when: When the site has no indexable content or is deliberately excluded from Google Search via robots.txt or noindex directives, making visual element optimisation irrelevant.
source_title: Visual Elements Gallery – Google Search Result Building Blocks

name: Optimise Content for Google AI Mode Visibility
summary: A structured process for optimising web content to appear as cited sources in Google AI Mode responses, focusing on comprehensive topic coverage, comparative content, and entity clarity.
use_when: When a site owner wants to increase the likelihood of being cited in Google AI Mode responses, particularly for complex, multi-step, or comparative queries in their domain.
avoid_when: When the site primarily targets short, high-volume head queries with no comparative or multi-part content needs, or when the site does not yet meet standard Google indexing and snippet eligibility requirements.
source_title: Google AI Mode How It Works and Optimisation

name: Video SEO Eligibility and Rich Feature Setup
summary: Configure video pages and technical settings to make videos eligible for Google Search video features including main results, Video mode, Key Moments, and Live Badge.
use_when: When publishing videos on a website and wanting them to appear in Google Search video features, rich results, or Key Moments.
avoid_when: When videos are behind authentication, blocked by robots.txt or noindex, or loaded via URL fragments that Google cannot process.
source_title: Video SEO Best Practices for Google Search

name: Video Sitemap Creation and Submission
summary: Step-by-step process to create and submit a video sitemap (or mRSS feed alternative) so Google can discover and understand video content on a site.
use_when: When a site hosts video content, especially newly added videos or videos that Google may not discover through standard crawling, and you want to ensure they are indexed.
avoid_when: When videos are unrelated to the content of the host page, or when video files cannot be made accessible to Googlebot (e.g., blocked by robots.txt, behind a login, or only available via streaming protocols).
source_title: Video Sitemaps and Alternatives

name: Video Sitemap Implementation for Google Discovery
summary: Create and configure a video sitemap (or mRSS feed alternative) so Google can find, understand, and index video content on your site.
use_when: You have video content on your site that Google may not be discovering, or you have recently added video content and want to accelerate indexing.
avoid_when: Videos are hosted on third-party platforms where you don't control the host page, or videos are unrelated to the content of the page they appear on.
source_title: Video Sitemaps and Alternatives

name: Enable Web Stories on Google Search
summary: Step-by-step procedure to create, validate, and get Web Stories indexed on Google Search and Discover feed.
use_when: Publishing a new Web Story and needing it to appear in Google Search results or the Discover feed.
avoid_when: The content is text-heavy (more than 180 characters per page), purely promotional, or requires users to leave the story to access essential information.
source_title: Web Stories – Enable, Best Practices, and Content Policy

name: Signal Canonical URL to Google
summary: Use available signals to indicate your preferred canonical URL to Google, reducing duplicate content issues in search indexing.
use_when: Your site has duplicate or near-duplicate content across multiple URLs due to region variants, device variants, HTTP/HTTPS protocol differences, sorting/filtering parameters, or accidentally accessible demo pages.
avoid_when: Pages have different primary language content — Google treats these as distinct pages, not duplicates requiring canonicalization.
source_title: What is URL Canonicalization

name: Ecommerce Content Visibility Optimization for Google Surfaces
summary: A checklist for ecommerce site owners to ensure their content appears across all relevant Google surfaces by preparing each type of content Google can surface for shoppers.
use_when: When setting up or auditing an ecommerce site to maximize visibility across Google Search, Google Images, Google Lens, the Shopping tab, Google Maps, and Business Profile.
avoid_when: When the site is not ecommerce-focused or does not sell products/services directly to consumers.
source_title: Where Ecommerce Content Can Appear on Google

name: Implement VideoObject Structured Data for Video Rich Results
summary: Add VideoObject schema.org markup to video pages so Google can display rich results with thumbnail, duration, upload date, and other video details in search.
use_when: A webpage contains a video and the goal is to enable video rich results in Google Search with metadata like thumbnail, duration, and upload date.
avoid_when: The page does not contain video content, or the video is purely decorative/background with no meaningful content to describe.
source_title: Video Structured Data – VideoObject, Clip, and BroadcastEvent

name: Bing/Copilot Citation Eligibility Setup
summary: Configure a website to be indexed by Bing and eligible for citation in Microsoft Copilot and ChatGPT Browse mode by setting up Webmaster Tools, submitting sitemaps, configuring robots directives, enabling IndexNow, and auditing meta tags.
use_when: When launching a new site or auditing an existing site for AI citation eligibility in Bing-powered systems (Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT Browse mode), or when content is indexed but not appearing as Copilot citations.
avoid_when: When the site intentionally restricts AI indexing or citation (e.g., paywalled content, confidential internal pages, or sites with legal reasons to block AI grounding).
source_title: Bing Webmaster Guidelines AI and Copilot Grounding

name: IndexNow Implementation for Real-Time AI Search Visibility
summary: Set up and use the IndexNow protocol to notify Bing and other participating search engines immediately when content is published or updated, ensuring faster indexing and citation eligibility in AI answer engines like Microsoft Copilot and ChatGPT Browse mode.
use_when: Content changes frequently or freshness determines citation relevance — e.g., news articles, product pricing pages, regulatory updates, seasonal information, or any page where delayed indexing means outdated AI citations.
avoid_when: Changes are cosmetic only (reformatting, design updates, metadata-only changes) or the same URL has already been submitted recently without substantive content changes. Also avoid using this as a Google indexing strategy — IndexNow does not participate with Google.
source_title: Bing IndexNow Real Time Indexing for AI Visibility

name: Google AI Overviews Technical Eligibility Checklist
summary: A step-by-step technical implementation process to ensure web pages are eligible to appear as supporting links in Google AI Overviews by satisfying all crawlability, indexability, and snippet requirements.
use_when: When optimizing a website or specific pages to be eligible for inclusion as supporting links in Google AI Overviews, or when diagnosing why pages are not appearing in AI Overviews.
avoid_when: When the page is intentionally gated behind a login, paywalled, or contains content that should not be publicly indexed or surfaced in search snippets.
source_title: Google AI Overviews Eligibility and Technical Requirements

name: Implement ListItem Structured Data for BreadcrumbList or Carousel
summary: Correctly structure ListItem markup within a BreadcrumbList or ItemList carousel, ensuring position, item, name, and url properties meet schema.org requirements for rich results.
use_when: Adding structured data markup for breadcrumb navigation or a rich result carousel where each entry must be represented as a ListItem with ordinal positioning.
avoid_when: The list does not require positional ordering or when implementing standalone Thing markup outside of an ItemList or BreadcrumbList context.
source_title: ListItem

name: Control AI Overviews Appearance Using Robots Meta Tags
summary: Configure robots meta tags to control whether and how your content appears in Google AI Overviews and AI Mode citations, using the same directives that govern standard Search snippet behavior.
use_when: When you want to explicitly allow, restrict, or fine-tune how specific pages or page sections appear in Google AI Overviews and AI Mode citation responses.
avoid_when: When targeting Bing Copilot or other AI search engines that use different directive systems; Google-specific robots meta tag configurations may not translate to those platforms.
source_title: Google AI Overviews Robots Meta Tag Controls

name: AI-Generated Content Compliance Workflow for Google Search
summary: A structured process for publishing AI-generated content that meets Google's quality, E-E-A-T, and spam policy requirements for both standard Search and AI Overviews.
use_when: When publishing AI-generated or AI-assisted content intended to appear in Google Search results, AI Overviews, or AI Mode, including text, images, and ecommerce product data.
avoid_when: When content is being produced purely for human audiences with no intent to index in Google Search, or when content has already been fully reviewed and published without any AI involvement.
source_title: Google AI Overviews Generative AI Content Policy

name: Implement LocalBusiness Schema Markup for Local SEO
summary: Add structured LocalBusiness schema markup to a business website to reinforce Google Business Profile data, improve local pack inclusion, and enable knowledge panel display.
use_when: Setting up or auditing schema markup for a physical commercial establishment, especially when targeting local search visibility, Google local pack, or business knowledge panels.
avoid_when: The business has no physical location, is a purely digital/online entity, or when a more specific subtype (Restaurant, Hotel, Dentist, etc.) already covers the entity — in that case use the subtype directly instead of bare LocalBusiness.
source_title: LocalBusiness

name: Implement FAQPage Schema Markup for AEO
summary: A step-by-step procedure for correctly implementing FAQPage JSON-LD structured data on a webpage to maximise eligibility for featured snippets, AI Overviews, and Perplexity citations.
