use_when: When a webpage displays visible question-answer content authored by the site owner and you want to improve AI answer engine citation probability and rich result eligibility.
avoid_when: When Q&A content is crowdsourced or user-submitted (use QAPage instead), when Q&A is hidden or dynamically loaded and not visible to users, or when fewer than 3 Q&A pairs exist on the page.
source_title: Schema FAQPage AEO Deep Dive

name: Implement Schema.org HowTo Structured Data for AEO
summary: Add HowTo JSON-LD markup to step-by-step instructional content to make it extractable by AI answer engines, eligible for Google rich results, and optimised for voice search responses.
use_when: The page contains sequential, task-completion instructional content with defined steps that are visibly present on the page, and the goal is to appear in AI Overviews, voice search, or Google HowTo rich results.
avoid_when: Content is a list of tips, general advice, evergreen reference material, or non-sequential content — HowTo markup is not appropriate for these formats. Also avoid if steps are hidden or dynamically loaded.
source_title: Schema HowTo AEO Application

name: Implement Schema.org Action (SearchAction) for Sitelinks Searchbox
summary: Add structured Action markup, specifically SearchAction as a potentialAction on a WebSite entity, to enable Google Sitelinks Searchbox and other action-based features.
use_when: When implementing structured data for a website that has internal search functionality and you want to enable Google Sitelinks Searchbox or support app deep linking and other potentialAction features.
avoid_when: When the site lacks a functional search endpoint or when the action type needed is too generic to warrant using bare Action instead of a specific subtype.
source_title: Action

name: Implement AggregateOffer Schema Markup for Multi-Seller Product Pages
summary: Structured procedure for adding AggregateOffer schema.org markup to product pages that aggregate pricing from multiple sellers or merchants.
use_when: A product page displays pricing or offers from multiple sellers, such as a price comparison page, marketplace listing, or aggregator page where a price range needs to be communicated to search engines.
avoid_when: A product is sold by only one seller or merchant — use a standard Offer schema instead. Also avoid when individual seller attribution is not needed and offer count is unknown.
source_title: AggregateOffer

name: Implement AggregateRating Schema for Rich Result Star Ratings
summary: Add valid AggregateRating structured data to product, business, or service pages to enable star rating display in Google Rich Results and improve click-through rate.
use_when: A page features real user ratings or reviews for a Product, LocalBusiness, CreativeWork, Event, or Service and you want to display star ratings in Google search results.
avoid_when: The page does not have actual user-generated ratings or reviews, or when ratings are fabricated or artificially inflated — Google penalises suspicious rating patterns.
source_title: AggregateRating

name: Implement QAPage Schema Markup for Community Q&A Pages
summary: Apply Schema.org QAPage structured data to community-generated Q&A pages to enable Google rich results and AI answer engine citation.
use_when: A page hosts a single community question with multiple user-contributed answers, such as forum threads, Stack Overflow-style pages, Reddit posts, or community help pages where the site owner does not write all answers.
avoid_when: The page contains pre-written authoritative answers by the site owner (use FAQPage instead), hosts multiple questions or topic listings, or contains a multi-topic thread not centered on a single question.
source_title: Schema QAPage AEO Application

name: Implement Schema.org Book Markup for Rich Results
summary: Add structured Schema.org Book markup to book pages to enable Google Rich Results displaying covers, authors, and reviews in search.
use_when: When publishing, selling, cataloging, or reviewing books online and wanting to enable Google Rich Results for book pages
avoid_when: When the content is not a published book (e.g., articles, whitepapers, or non-book creative works) or when the page lacks at minimum a book title and author
source_title: Book

name: Monitor AI Overview (AIO) Presence and Trends via Brand Radar
summary: A step-by-step process to track AI Overview counts, search volume exposure, and trend changes using Brand Radar and Big Data reference pages.
use_when: When conducting ongoing SEO performance monitoring and you need to quantify AIO presence across your tracked keyword set and assess traffic impact risk.
avoid_when: When analyzing branded queries, local queries, or monetized/commercial searches where AIOs rarely appear — results will not be meaningful.
source_title: Insights From 55 8M AI Overviews Across 590M Searches

name: Implement Article Schema Markup for Rich Results Eligibility
summary: Add structured Article schema.org markup to editorial content pages to qualify for Google Rich Results and Top Stories features, covering all required and recommended properties.
use_when: Adding or auditing structured data on news articles, blog posts, opinion pieces, investigative reports, or other editorial content pages that should be eligible for Google Rich Results or Top Stories carousel.
avoid_when: The content is not editorial in nature (e.g., product pages, event listings, FAQs) or when a more specific subtype such as NewsArticle, BlogPosting, or TechArticle is more appropriate for the content category.
source_title: Article

name: Implement BlogPosting Schema Markup
summary: Add structured data markup for a blog post page using the BlogPosting schema type to enable Google Rich Results eligibility and improve semantic clarity.
use_when: Adding or auditing structured data on a blog post page that has a headline, publication date, and identifiable author, and you want to qualify for Google Article Rich Results.
avoid_when: The content is a real-time updating post (e.g., live sports commentary or breaking news live blog) — use LiveBlogPosting instead. Also avoid if the content is a formal news article without a personal/informal editorial voice — use Article or NewsArticle instead.
source_title: BlogPosting

name: Implement BreadcrumbList Schema for Rich Results
summary: Structure and embed BreadcrumbList JSON-LD markup on a webpage to enable Google breadcrumb rich results in search snippets, improving click-through rate by displaying the navigation path instead of a raw URL.
use_when: Adding structured data to any webpage that has a hierarchical navigation path (breadcrumb trail) and you want to enable Google Rich Results for breadcrumbs in search snippets.
avoid_when: The page has no meaningful hierarchical navigation path, or the current page is already referenced via WebPage.breadcrumb and adding a separate BreadcrumbList would duplicate it.
source_title: BreadcrumbList

name: Implement Course List Structured Data for Google Rich Results
summary: Add schema.org Course and ItemList structured data to enable course list rich results in Google Search, covering both the summary/list page and individual course detail pages.
use_when: When a site hosts multiple courses and wants them to appear as a host carousel rich result in Google Search, requiring both a summary listing page and individual course detail pages.
avoid_when: When the site only has a single course page with no list/summary page, or when the courses are not in English or the target region does not have Google Search availability.
source_title: Course List Structured Data – Course Discoverability on Google

name: Implement Schema.org Course Markup for Google Rich Results
summary: Add structured Course schema markup to educational course pages to enable Google Rich Results displaying course title, provider, and description in search.
use_when: When publishing course pages on online learning platforms, university sites, professional training providers, or MOOC platforms that need Google Rich Results eligibility.
avoid_when: When the page describes a general subject area or curriculum catalog without a specific, enrollable course — or when no provider organization can be identified.
source_title: Course

name: Implement Schema.org Dataset Markup for Google Dataset Search
summary: Add structured Dataset schema markup to a dataset landing page to make it eligible for Google Dataset Search, including all required and recommended properties.
use_when: Publishing an open or licensed dataset on a research institution, government agency, or data repository site and wanting it to appear in Google Dataset Search.
avoid_when: The content is not a structured data collection (e.g., a blog post or product page), or the dataset is private/access-restricted with no public landing page.
source_title: Dataset

name: Implement Movie Schema Markup for Rich Results
summary: Add structured Schema.org Movie markup to film pages to enable Google Rich Results displaying cast, director, rating, and streaming availability in search.
use_when: Adding or updating structured data on pages representing individual movies, films, documentaries, or short films on streaming platforms, film databases, review sites, or cinema chain websites.
avoid_when: Marking up film franchises or series (use MovieSeries instead), or marking up non-film video content such as TV episodes or web series.
source_title: Movie

name: Implement CreativeWork Schema Markup
summary: Add structured Schema.org CreativeWork markup to content pages, providing all recommended properties for authorship, publication, licensing, and content metadata.
use_when: When adding schema markup to a creative content page (article, book, video, dataset, etc.) that does not clearly fit a more specific Schema.org subtype, or when building a base template for creative content entities.
avoid_when: When a more specific subtype clearly applies (e.g., Article, VideoObject, Recipe, Book) — use the specific subtype instead of the generic CreativeWork type.
source_title: CreativeWork

name: Implement EntryPoint Schema for Action Markup
summary: Add EntryPoint structured data as the target of an Action object to define how the action can be invoked, including URL templates, platform support, and content types.
use_when: Adding structured data for SearchAction (Sitelinks Searchbox), app deep linking, or API endpoint actions where Google or other consumers need to understand how to invoke an action.
avoid_when: When the action does not have a specific invocation mechanism or when the page does not support a user-triggered action (e.g., purely informational pages with no search or deep link functionality).
source_title: EntryPoint

name: Implement ImageObject Structured Markup for Images
summary: Add schema.org ImageObject structured data markup to image files to enable Google Images rich results, publisher logo display, and image entity recognition.
use_when: When adding structured metadata to images used in Articles, Organization logos, primary page images, or any context where image rich results or entity recognition are desired.
avoid_when: When the image lacks a direct accessible URL, or when the image is purely decorative with no semantic value to search engines.
source_title: ImageObject

name: Implement HowTo Schema Markup for Rich Results
summary: Mark up instructional content with Schema.org HowTo structured data to qualify for Google Rich Results that display step titles, images, and duration in search.
use_when: The page contains genuinely instructional, step-by-step content describing how to complete a task or achieve a goal, and you want to qualify for HowTo Rich Results in Google Search.
avoid_when: The content is not genuinely instructional or does not describe a task completion process — applying HowTo to non-instructional content violates schema guidelines.
source_title: HowTo

name: Select the Most Specific Schema.org Intangible Subtype
summary: When marking up intangible concepts (offers, services, bookings, etc.) with Schema.org, identify and apply the most specific subtype of Intangible rather than using the bare Intangible type.
use_when: Marking up non-physical concepts such as pricing, bookings, services, job postings, languages, tickets, or structured values in Schema.org structured data.
avoid_when: When no specific subtype matches the concept being described and a custom or external vocabulary is more appropriate.
source_title: Intangible

name: Implement JobPosting Structured Data for Google Jobs Rich Results
summary: Add schema.org JobPosting markup to job listing pages to qualify for Google Jobs rich results, including title, company, location, salary, and apply button in Google Search.
use_when: Publishing a new job listing on a recruiter site, job board, or company careers page that should appear in Google Jobs search results.
avoid_when: The job posting has already expired (validThrough date has passed) or the listing is no longer accepting applications, as expired listings are removed from Google Jobs.
source_title: JobPosting

name: Implement ItemList Schema for Google Carousel Rich Results
summary: Add ItemList structured data markup to a page to enable Google's host carousel rich results, displaying a visually scrollable list of items like recipes, articles, or courses in search results.
use_when: When a page contains a list of homogeneous items (recipes, articles, courses, restaurants) that individually support Google rich results and you want to enable carousel display in search results.
avoid_when: When items in the list are of mixed rich result types, when individual items do not have their own pages with rich result markup, or when the list is a breadcrumb (use BreadcrumbList instead).
source_title: ItemList

name: Implement Organization Schema Markup for Entity Recognition
summary: Add structured Organization schema markup to a website to enable entity recognition in knowledge graphs, support E-E-A-T signals, and qualify for rich results.
use_when: When marking up a company, school, NGO, government body, club, or any organized group of people that needs to be recognized as an entity by search engines and knowledge graphs.
avoid_when: When the entity is a physical commercial establishment (shop, restaurant, etc.) — use LocalBusiness subtype instead of bare Organization in those cases.
source_title: Organization

name: Implement PriceSpecification Schema Markup
summary: Add structured PriceSpecification markup to an Offer or Demand entity to express complex pricing conditions including currency, validity periods, quantity eligibility, and price ranges.
use_when: When marking up product or service pricing that involves complex conditions such as quantity requirements, price ranges, volume discounts, time-limited pricing, or unit-based pricing (e.g., price per kilogram).
avoid_when: When pricing is simple and already fully expressed via a plain numeric price property on an Offer — adding PriceSpecification may be unnecessary overhead. Also avoid when price data is unavailable or not reliably maintained, as stale validThrough dates can cause rich result eligibility issues.
source_title: PriceSpecification

name: Google Crawler Compliance Classification Procedure
summary: Classify Google crawlers/fetchers into the correct category to apply appropriate robots.txt and compliance rules
use_when: When configuring robots.txt rules, server access controls, or crawler policies for a website that receives Google crawler traffic and needs to differentiate treatment by crawler type
avoid_when: When dealing with non-Google crawlers or when a blanket block of all crawlers is acceptable without differentiation
source_title: Google Crawler User Agent Overview

name: Implement Schema.org Person Markup for E-E-A-T and Knowledge Graph
summary: Add structured Person schema markup to establish authorship, build knowledge graph entities, and support E-E-A-T signals for named individuals on biographical pages, author profiles, or social profiles.
use_when: When publishing author pages, contributor profiles, biographical pages, or any page representing a named individual where authorship and identity verification are important for SEO or E-E-A-T purposes.
avoid_when: When the subject is an organization rather than an individual person, or when no verified identity information (name, URL, or sameAs sources) is available to populate the markup meaningfully.
source_title: Person

name: Implement Schema.org Place Markup for Geographic Entity Recognition
summary: Add structured Place schema markup to a web page representing a physical location, including all required and recommended properties for GEO/local search optimization.
use_when: Adding structured data markup to a page representing a physical location such as a landmark, building, geographic region, or natural feature that does not qualify as a LocalBusiness.
avoid_when: The location is a commercial establishment — use LocalBusiness schema instead, which is a subtype of both Organization and Place.
source_title: Place

name: Implement SearchAction for Google Sitelinks Searchbox
summary: Add SearchAction schema markup to a website's homepage to enable the Google Sitelinks Searchbox feature, which displays a site-specific search box directly in Google search results.
use_when: When a website has its own search functionality and the site owner wants to enable or signal eligibility for Google's Sitelinks Searchbox feature in search results.
avoid_when: When the site's search URL pattern returns errors or empty results, as Google validates the search functionality and invalid endpoints may cause issues.
source_title: SearchAction

name: Implement Recipe Schema Markup for Rich Results
summary: Add structured Recipe schema.org markup to food and drink pages to qualify for Google rich results including rating stars, cooking time, calories, and image carousels.
use_when: Adding or auditing schema markup on recipe pages for food and drink content intended to appear in Google Search rich results, Google Images recipe filter, or Google Assistant recipe mode.
avoid_when: The page does not contain an actual recipe (e.g. a food blog post without preparation instructions, or a restaurant menu page).
source_title: Recipe

name: Implement Schema.org Review Markup for Rich Results
summary: Add structured Review markup to pages containing critical evaluations to enable Google Rich Results with star ratings and review snippets.
use_when: A page contains a review of a product, service, place, book, movie, or other item and you want to enable rich result snippets with star ratings in search.
avoid_when: The review is self-authored by the same entity being reviewed, or the review was not submitted by a genuine user — both violate Google review guidelines.
source_title: Review

name: Implement TechArticle Schema Markup for Technical Content
summary: Add structured TechArticle schema.org markup to technical content pages such as how-to guides, programming tutorials, and technical specifications to improve search indexing and developer-focused information retrieval.
use_when: Publishing technical content pages including API documentation, software how-to guides, programming tutorials, or technical specifications that need enhanced search categorisation.
avoid_when: Marking up formal API reference documentation pages, which should use the APIReference subtype instead of TechArticle.
source_title: TechArticle

name: Implement WebSite Schema Markup with Sitelinks Searchbox
summary: Add JSON-LD WebSite schema to a site's homepage to enable the Google Sitelinks Searchbox feature, allowing users to search the site directly from Google search results.
use_when: You are implementing structured data on a website homepage and want to enable the Google Sitelinks Searchbox feature or establish canonical site identity in search results.
avoid_when: You are adding schema markup to any page other than the homepage, or when working with individual page-level schema (use WebPage instead of WebSite for those cases).
source_title: WebSite

name: GEO Performance Measurement Setup
summary: Establish tracking and monitoring for brand performance across AI search platforms, covering citation frequency, share of voice, sentiment, and referred traffic.
use_when: When a brand has no visibility into how it appears in AI-generated search answers and needs to build a GEO measurement baseline.
avoid_when: When using traditional SEO tools only — these cannot track AI citation metrics and will not provide the required data.
source_title: Mastering generative engine optimization in 2026 Full guide Phase 3 Measure your AI search performan

name: Build an AI-Ready SEO Team: Phase 1 Foundation
summary: A structured 3-month foundation phase to assess current SEO team capabilities, establish AI search visibility baselines, run targeted pilots, and document learnings — enabling teams to evolve for AI-era search without massive reorganization.
use_when: An SEO team is beginning its transition toward AI search readiness and needs a structured starting framework to assess gaps, prioritize improvements, and test changes before scaling.
avoid_when: The team has already completed a capability audit and established AI visibility baselines — this is an entry-level foundation phase and would be redundant for teams already in later optimization stages.
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Phase 1 Foundation

name: Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) Implementation Playbook
summary: A structured process for optimizing content to be cited as an authoritative source by AI answer engines like ChatGPT, shifting focus from rankings to citations.
use_when: When a brand needs visibility in AI-generated answer surfaces (e.g., ChatGPT, AI Overviews) and wants to be cited as the authoritative source rather than just achieving traditional search rankings.
avoid_when: When the target queries are highly transactional or navigational with no realistic answer-engine citation opportunity, or when the site lacks the domain authority to reach top-three search results for target queries.
source_title: Answer Engine Optimization AEO The comprehensive guide for 2026 Shift from rankings to citations

name: AI Search Optimization: Topic Testing and Content Structuring
summary: A systematic process for identifying what content AI platforms prefer to cite, then restructuring existing and new content to match that format for maximum AI search visibility.
use_when: Content creators or SEO professionals want their content cited by AI systems like Gemini, Perplexity, ChatGPT, or Google AI Overviews, especially when competitors are consistently appearing in AI results and you are not.
avoid_when: Content is highly contextual or narrative-driven and cannot be broken into standalone sections without losing meaning, or when the target audience does not use AI search tools.
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 3 Test Your Topics on AI Platforms

name: GEO Readiness Audit for AI-Powered Search Visibility
summary: A structured assessment process to evaluate whether your brand is being cited by AI-powered search platforms, identify gaps, and establish a baseline before optimization efforts begin.
use_when: When a brand has no existing GEO strategy, is launching a new optimization initiative, or wants to benchmark current AI search visibility before making changes.
avoid_when: When a baseline audit has already been completed and the brand is already in an active GEO optimization cycle — skip directly to optimization and iteration steps instead.
source_title: Mastering generative engine optimization in 2026 Full guide How to get your brand cited recommended

name: AI Overview Citation Optimization Playbook
summary: A four-step process to analyze existing AI Overview citation patterns, identify competitor gaps, and strengthen pages to increase selection as AI Overview sources.
use_when: When a site is ranking in top 10 results but not being cited in AI Overviews, or when you want to expand AI Overview citation visibility beyond current rankings.
avoid_when: When you have insufficient data on AI Overview citations for your domain, or when your site has no existing AI Overview citations to establish baseline trust signals.
source_title: Update 38 of AI Overview Citations Pull From The Top 10 3

name: Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) Implementation Playbook
summary: A practical approach to optimizing content for AI-generated answers while maintaining value for human readers, complementing existing SEO and digital marketing efforts.
use_when: A brand wants to improve visibility in AI-generated search answers (e.g., ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity) and needs a foundational GEO content strategy.
avoid_when: You are considering replacing traditional SEO, social media, email marketing, or paid ads entirely with GEO, or when content quality for human readers would be sacrificed.
source_title: Generative Engine Optimization A Practical Guide Frequently Asked Questions About GEO

name: Verify and Optimize AI Crawler Access to Your Content
summary: A step-by-step process to confirm AI search crawlers can access your content and that your pages are optimized with specific, sourced statistics to increase the likelihood of AI citations.
use_when: Your content is not appearing as a source in AI platforms like ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Claude, or when beginning an AI search optimization initiative for the first time.
avoid_when: Your content is behind mandatory paywalls or login walls that cannot be removed, making crawler access structurally impossible without a product change.
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 1 Verify AI Systems Can Access Your Content

name: GEO Iterate and Scale Workflow
summary: A continuous improvement cycle for Generative Engine Optimization that uses performance data to identify citation-earning content and scales successful approaches across formats and platforms.
use_when: After initial GEO content and technical foundation is in place and performance data is available across AI platforms, requiring systematic scaling of what works.
avoid_when: When no baseline GEO implementation or performance tracking exists yet — foundational setup (Phases 1-3) must be completed before iterating and scaling.
source_title: Mastering generative engine optimization in 2026 Full guide Phase 4 Iterate and scale

name: GEO Content Optimization for AI Engines
summary: A structured procedure for optimizing web content so AI engines (like ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity) can accurately parse, cite, and surface it in generative responses. Covers content structure, entity authority, technical foundations, and content freshness.
use_when: When publishing or auditing content intended to appear in AI-generated answers, when building a GEO strategy, or when existing content is losing visibility to newer competitor articles on the same topic.
avoid_when: When the site is not yet technically sound (poor load times, broken architecture, mobile issues) — fix technical foundations first before investing in GEO content optimization.
source_title: Mastering generative engine optimization in 2026 Full guide Phase 2 Optimize your content for AI eng

name: Make Content Citation-Worthy for AI Search Results
summary: A structured process to transform generic content into AI-citable material by adding specific data points, expert credentials, and concrete examples that AI systems can extract and reference.
use_when: When optimizing existing or new content to improve visibility and citation frequency in AI-generated search results and summaries.
avoid_when: When content is time-sensitive breaking news where data and case studies are not yet available, or when expecting immediate AI visibility improvements rather than long-term foundational gains.
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 5 Make Yourself Easy to Quote

name: YouTube-First AI Overview Citation Optimization Playbook
summary: A process for optimizing brand presence on YouTube and aligning content strategy around fan-out queries to increase AI Overview citation rates.
use_when: When a brand wants to increase its visibility in Google AI Overviews and has not yet incorporated YouTube content into its AI search strategy, or when existing content is optimized for keywords rather than fan-out queries and user journeys.
avoid_when: When the brand lacks the resources to produce YouTube content or when the goal is purely traditional SERP ranking without concern for AIO citations, since ranking in the same SERP as an AIO is no longer sufficient for citation.
source_title: Update 38 of AI Overview Citations Pull From The Top 10 2

name: Measure AI Overview CTR Impact on Your Rankings
summary: A research methodology to quantify how AI Overviews are reducing clickthrough rates for your specific keyword rankings using Ahrefs and Google Search Console data.
use_when: When you need to assess the business impact of AI Overviews on your site's organic traffic, prioritize which keyword rankings to defend or deprioritize, or benchmark your CTR losses against industry findings.
avoid_when: When you lack access to Google Search Console data or Ahrefs Site Explorer, or when your keyword set is too small to produce statistically meaningful comparisons.
source_title: Update AI Overviews Reduce Clicks by 58

name: Content Freshness Update for AI Search Visibility
summary: A prioritized content refresh process to improve visibility in AI-powered search tools by updating pages with recent statistics, modern context, and original data.
use_when: Pages that were previously high-traffic are losing visibility, or when AI Overviews and ChatGPT citations are favoring competitors with more recent but lower-quality content.
avoid_when: Pages that are already recently published or consistently generating strong AI search visibility — avoid unnecessary churn that could disrupt well-performing content.
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 6 Keep Your Content Updated

name: AI Search Optimization Quick-Start Implementation
summary: A structured 5-step process for marketers and SEO professionals to begin optimizing brand visibility in AI search results, starting with free manual testing before scaling to paid tools.
use_when: When a brand needs to improve visibility in AI-generated responses on platforms like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini, and is starting from scratch or has fewer than 20 topics to monitor.
avoid_when: When you need to monitor 20+ topics and subtopics consistently, require competitor benchmarking at scale, need historical trend data, or require automated alert systems for brand mention changes — use paid monitoring tools instead.
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 Tools That Help You Optimize for AI Search

name: AI Search Optimization (AEO) Content Restructuring
summary: Restructure existing content to be optimized for AI search engines that cite and synthesize information, rather than traditional search engines that rank and link pages.
use_when: When a site's content is written for traditional SEO keyword rankings and needs to be adapted for AI-driven search systems that act as intermediaries and synthesize information from multiple sources.
avoid_when: When the primary goal is direct traffic volume from traditional search engines, or when the audience relies exclusively on non-AI search tools.
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 How AI Search Differs from Traditional SEO

name: AI Search Optimization (GEO) Implementation Playbook
summary: A structured process for optimizing content to be frequently cited and prominently featured by AI-powered search platforms including Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT Search, Perplexity, and Microsoft Copilot.
use_when: When a content creator or SEO practitioner wants their content to appear as a preferred citation in AI-generated responses rather than (or in addition to) traditional search engine results.
avoid_when: When the primary goal is ranking in traditional organic search results pages without any AI search presence, or when the content is not publicly indexable by AI platforms.
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 What Is AI Search Optimization

name: AEO Success Measurement Setup
summary: A step-by-step process to measure Answer Engine Optimization effectiveness by tracking AI citations, branded query volume, and search impressions across platforms.
use_when: When you have implemented or are implementing an AEO strategy and need to establish baseline visibility metrics, track brand mentions in AI-generated responses, and monitor branded search volume in Google Search Console.
avoid_when: When you need comprehensive, scalable AI mention monitoring at volume — manual checking alone is insufficient and a dedicated tool like Semrush's AI Visibility Toolkit should supplement this process.
source_title: What Is Answer Engine Optimization And How to Do It How to Measure AEO Success

name: Rank in AI Overviews: Keyword Research and Content Targeting Workflow
summary: A step-by-step process to identify and target keywords that trigger AI Overviews, increasing the likelihood of being cited in AI-generated search responses.
use_when: When a site already has pages ranking in the traditional top 10 and wants to optimize for AI Overview citations, particularly for informational content strategies.
avoid_when: When targeting transactional or navigational queries, or when the site has no existing top-10 rankings to build from, as AI Overview citations are strongly correlated with top-10 organic positions.
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 1

name: AI Overviews Ranking Optimization Playbook
summary: A data-backed procedure to optimize content for inclusion in AI Overviews by prioritizing query-aligned, intent-focused content over length, using specific tools to diagnose intent and write accordingly.
use_when: When a page is ranking in the top 10 but not appearing in AI Overviews, or when creating new content intended to be cited by AI Overviews for a target keyword.
avoid_when: When the page is sitting outside the top 10 organic results — fix ranking first before optimizing for AI Overview inclusion.
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 2

name: Fan-Out Query Optimization for AI Overview Rankings
summary: A systematic process to identify and optimize content around Google's AI Overview fan-out sub-queries, increasing the probability of being cited in AI Overviews by building deep topical authority across related queries.
use_when: You want to increase citation frequency in Google AI Overviews, have an existing content library (e.g., a /blog/ subfolder), and want to identify topical gaps that prevent broad fan-out query coverage.
avoid_when: Your site lacks sufficient existing content to analyze, or you are focused solely on short-tail keyword rankings without intent to build topical depth.
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 3

name: Entity Clustering and Brand Mention Strategy for AI Overview Visibility
summary: A structured process to rank in AI Overviews by building entity clusters, strengthening internal linking, and securing authentic brand mentions across authoritative sites rather than targeting single keywords.
use_when: When a site is absent from or underperforming in AI Overview citations and needs a systematic approach to improve entity association and brand recognition across SERPs.
avoid_when: When the site lacks sufficient existing content to cluster, or when targeting single transactional keywords without a broader topical authority strategy in place.
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 4

name: AI Overview Citation Pattern Analysis
summary: A structured process for analyzing which URLs appear in Google AI Overview citations versus standard SERP positions, accounting for SERP features and organic listings as separate blocks.
use_when: When auditing a site's AI Overview visibility, diagnosing why certain pages are or are not cited in AI Overviews, or benchmarking citation patterns across a keyword set.
avoid_when: When working with branded or navigational queries where AI Overviews rarely appear, or when the keyword set is too small to yield statistically meaningful citation patterns.

avoid_when: When publishing to a single platform with no cross-posting needs, or when platform-specific nuance requires fully customized posts that cannot be templated.

avoid_when: When a site lacks sufficient traffic or crawl volume to generate meaningful AI crawler log data, making analysis statistically unreliable.
source_title: A Step By Step Guide For Growing AI Engine Engagement

name: On-Page SEO: Time-Sensitive Title Optimization and UX Formatting
summary: A structured process for adding current year signals to titles/descriptions and optimizing on-page UX to improve CTR and reader engagement for time-sensitive content.
use_when: Publishing or updating content where searchers expect current information (e.g., product roundups, software comparisons, how-to guides with evolving best practices) and CTR improvement is a goal.
avoid_when: Content is evergreen and time-agnostic (e.g., philosophical concepts, historical topics) where adding a year adds no value and may date the content unnecessarily.
source_title: On Page SEO The Definitive Guide FREE Template 2026 Add the Current Year to Your Title and Descripti

name: On-Page SEO Continuous Monitoring and Adaptation Workflow
summary: A repeatable process for monitoring on-page SEO performance metrics and adapting content and metadata to maintain search visibility over time.
use_when: When a website has existing on-page SEO optimizations in place and requires ongoing performance tracking to respond to algorithm changes, declining rankings, or shifts in user behavior.
avoid_when: When a website has not yet completed initial on-page SEO implementation; monitoring before baseline optimizations are in place will not yield actionable signals.
source_title: On Page SEO The Definitive Guide FREE Template 2026 Regularly Monitor and Adapt Your On Page SEO Eff

name: Search Everywhere Optimization (SEvO) Diversification Playbook
summary: Reposition SEO strategy away from traditional Google-centric informational content toward a multi-platform, multi-channel approach that ensures discoverability across all platforms where audiences search, including social, video, and AI assistants.
use_when: When organic traffic is declining due to AI Overviews, when top-funnel informational content is being cannibalized by AI summarization, or when a brand is over-indexed on traditional Google SEO with no multi-channel presence.
avoid_when: When a brand operates in a niche where AI Overviews have minimal impact (e.g., highly transactional or local queries) or when resources are insufficient to maintain quality across multiple platforms simultaneously.
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 4

name: Adapt SEO Strategy for Zero-Click AI Search Environment
summary: Reorient SEO efforts toward Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) and transactional content to maintain visibility and traffic in an environment where AI Overviews and AI Mode capture informational queries with near-zero click-through rates.
use_when: When an existing SEO strategy relies heavily on informational content traffic, organic click-through from AI Overview citations, or top-of-funnel discovery through Google search, and site owners are seeing traffic declines due to AI-generated answers.
avoid_when: When the site operates exclusively in a niche where AI Overviews are not yet prevalent, or when the business model does not depend on organic search traffic (e.g., direct traffic or paid channels only).
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 2

name: Image Optimization for SEO and AEO
summary: A step-by-step process to optimize images on a webpage for search engine visibility, page load speed, and LLM context signals through descriptive filenames, alt text, and file optimization.
use_when: When publishing or auditing web pages that contain images and you want to improve SEO visibility, image search presence, page load speed, and contextual signals for LLMs and search engines.
avoid_when: When images are purely decorative with no contextual relevance to the page topic, or when alt text would require keyword stuffing to be relevant.
source_title: On Page SEO The Definitive Guide FREE Template 2026 Optimize Your Images

name: Subscribe to Google Crawling Documentation Updates Feed
summary: Set up feed access to Google's crawling documentation changelog to stay informed about changes to crawling behavior, new user agents, and related updates.
use_when: When a site owner or SEO practitioner needs to monitor Google's crawling documentation for changes that may affect crawler verification processes or crawling behavior.
avoid_when: When the site owner already has an active subscription to the RSS feed and verification processes are already up to date.
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog March 2026

name: Optimize Title Tags for Click-Worthy SEO Performance
summary: A structured process for creating and auditing title tags that maximize ranking potential and click-through rates by following character limits, keyword positioning, and modifier usage.
use_when: When creating new pages, auditing existing page titles, or addressing duplicate/missing title tags identified in a site crawl.
avoid_when: When Google has already rewritten a title tag to a preferred version that outperforms your original, indicating the rewrite may be more contextually appropriate.
source_title: On Page SEO The Definitive Guide FREE Template 2026 Make Click Worthy Titles

name: On-Page SEO Implementation Playbook
summary: A structured process for optimizing individual webpages for search engines, AI platforms/LLMs, and users to improve visibility, relevance, and user experience.
use_when: When creating or auditing a webpage that needs to rank in traditional search engines and be cited by AI platforms, particularly when targeting specific keywords or improving page-level SEO signals.
avoid_when: When the primary goal is off-page SEO improvement (e.g., link building, brand mentions) or when the page is not intended to be indexed or discovered via search.
source_title: On Page SEO The Definitive Guide FREE Template 2026 What Is On Page SEO

name: AI Visibility Optimization Playbook
summary: A step-by-step process to monitor, analyze, and improve your brand's presence in AI-generated search results through citations and mentions.
use_when: When a brand wants to increase how often it appears in AI-generated answers, summaries, or recommendations across major AI search indexes.
avoid_when: When a brand has no existing organic search presence or content foundation — AI visibility builds on existing SEO, so foundational SEO work should come first.
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 3

name: AI Search Visibility Optimization Playbook
summary: A strategic procedure for optimizing a website or brand to gain citations and visibility in AI-powered search results, targeting the early-mover advantage before the channel becomes saturated.
use_when: When a site owner wants to capture AI search traffic while the channel is undercrowded, especially if the site struggles to compete in traditional saturated SERPs or wants to accelerate brand-driven revenue growth.
avoid_when: When the target audience demonstrably does not use AI search tools, or when the business lacks any brand presence or content foundation to build AI citations from.
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 Why AI Visibility Is the Next SEO Frontier

name: On-Page SEO: Search Intent Alignment and Content Optimization
summary: A structured process to ensure your content matches search intent, uses proper formatting for AI and traditional search engines, and includes all on-page optimization elements needed to rank on page one.
use_when: When creating or optimizing a page targeting a specific keyword and aiming for first-page Google rankings, especially when existing content is ranking below page two.
avoid_when: When the target keyword's first page is dominated 100% by a content format you cannot produce (e.g., all results are tools and you can only publish blog posts), as ranking potential is effectively zero regardless of optimization.
source_title: On Page SEO The Definitive Guide FREE Template 2026 Satisfy Search Intent

name: Diagnose and Fix High Exit Rate Pages Using Google Analytics
summary: A procedure for identifying pages with problematically high Exit Rates, distinguishing them from Bounce Rate issues, and determining whether the exit rate indicates a real problem or expected user behavior (e.g., task completion).
use_when: When analyzing Google Analytics data to identify underperforming pages, or when a specific page shows a notably high Exit Rate that may be hurting conversions or user journeys.
avoid_when: When the high Exit Rate is on a page where task completion is the expected outcome (e.g., contact confirmation pages, thank-you pages), as high exit rates there may be positive signals rather than problems.
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Bounce Rate vs Exit Rate

name: Bounce Rate Audit and Improvement Workflow
summary: A structured process to identify underperforming pages using Google Analytics and PageSpeed Insights, then apply targeted content, technical, and UX improvements to reduce bounce rate.
use_when: Site is experiencing high bounce rates, low average engagement time, or declining SEO rankings due to poor user engagement signals.
avoid_when: Site has insufficient traffic data to produce statistically meaningful engagement metrics, or when bounce rate is already at or below industry benchmarks for the niche.
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It How to Improve Your Bounce Rate

name: Broken Link Building via Wikipedia and Semrush
summary: A systematic process to identify broken external links using Wikipedia dead link tags and Semrush tools, then leverage those broken pages to earn backlinks by offering replacement content to sites currently linking to the dead resource.
use_when: When running a link building campaign and seeking scalable opportunities by finding pages that have already lost their linked resources, particularly in competitive niches where direct outreach is difficult.
avoid_when: When attempting to place links directly on Wikipedia pages — Wikipedia editors remove undeserving links within hours and all Wikipedia links are nofollow, providing no direct SEO value.
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 2

name: AI Search Visibility Optimization Playbook
summary: A structured process for brands to gain citations and mentions in AI-generated search results by optimizing content, consistency, and monitoring performance across LLMs and traditional search engines.
use_when: When a brand wants to improve visibility in AI-powered search tools and language models, or when competitors are appearing in AI-generated responses and the brand is not.
avoid_when: When the brand's website is not publicly accessible or indexed, as AI models cannot access and process gated or private content — foundational technical SEO issues must be resolved first.
source_title: Feeling Behind on AI Search How to Catch Up in 2026 What Can I Do to Appear in AI Search

name: Access Meta Tags Used by Google Read Aloud User Agent
summary: Procedure to access and inspect the meta tags that Google Read Aloud uses when crawling a page, based on the updated Google Read Aloud user agent documentation.
use_when: When you need to verify which meta tags Google Read Aloud reads on a page, or when troubleshooting why Google Read Aloud is or is not accessing specific page content.
avoid_when: When the site does not serve audio content or is not concerned with Google Read Aloud crawl behavior.
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog December 2025

name: Reduce Bounce Rate by Aligning Landing Pages with User Intent
summary: A structured approach to diagnosing and fixing high bounce rates by ensuring landing pages match user expectations, look trustworthy, and provide easy navigation.
use_when: A page has a high bounce rate and users are not engaging further, especially when traffic comes from paid ads, search results, or referral links pointing to specific content.
avoid_when: The page is intentionally a single-destination resource (e.g., a complete recipe, a contact page, or a calculator) where users are expected to get what they need and leave — a bounce here may indicate success, not failure.
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Why Do People Bounce

name: Early Keyword Placement in First 100 Words
summary: Place your target keyword naturally within the first 100-150 words of an article to signal topical relevance to Google and improve on-page SEO.
use_when: Writing or optimizing a new or existing article where a primary target keyword has been identified and on-page SEO needs to be strengthened.
avoid_when: The keyword cannot be placed naturally within the opening content without forcing awkward phrasing or disrupting the reader experience.
source_title: On Page SEO The Definitive Guide FREE Template 2026 Use Your Target Keyword in the First 100 Words

name: On-Page Keyword and URL Optimization Playbook
summary: A structured procedure for naturally incorporating target keywords into page content, URLs, and internal links to help Google understand page relevance without keyword stuffing.
use_when: When optimizing an existing page or creating a new page and need to implement on-page SEO best practices for keyword placement, URL structure, and internal linking.
avoid_when: When the page already has heavily optimized keyword placement and internal links in place, or when content is not yet drafted and keyword targets are undefined.
source_title: On Page SEO The Definitive Guide FREE Template 2026 Think About Keyword Frequency

name: Broken Link Building: Identify and Contact the Right Outreach Target
summary: A targeting procedure for broken link building campaigns that ensures outreach emails reach the person with authority to replace broken links, rather than generic inboxes.
use_when: When conducting a broken link building outreach campaign and you need to identify the correct contact person on a target website before sending replacement link requests.
avoid_when: When the target site has no staff page or identifiable editorial contacts, making role identification impossible, or when the site is too small to have a dedicated web/content manager.
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 4

name: Schema.org Conformance Implementation Playbook
summary: How to implement a schema.org validator or data checker that follows the pragmatic, flexible conformance model recommended by schema.org.
use_when: Building a schema.org validator, structured data checker, or markup parser that needs to handle real-world, imperfect schema.org implementations without rejecting valid but unconventional markup.
avoid_when: Building a strict RDF ontology validator or a system that requires formal type/property associations and cannot tolerate ambiguous or loosely typed structured data.
source_title: Data model   Schema org Conformance

name: Keyword-Optimized Heading Tags Implementation
summary: A structured process to ensure page headings (H1–H6) properly incorporate target keywords to signal topical relevance to search engines and LLMs while improving user experience.
use_when: When auditing or building a page that lacks proper H1 structure, is missing keyword-optimized headings, or needs improved topical relevance signals for SEO.
avoid_when: When headings are already properly structured with target keywords and an audit tool confirms no missing H1 tags across the site.
source_title: On Page SEO The Definitive Guide FREE Template 2026 Wrap Your Keywords into Headings

name: Meta Description Optimization Playbook
summary: Craft and implement effective meta descriptions for each page to maximize organic click-through rates while meeting character limits and content alignment requirements.
use_when: Creating new pages, auditing existing pages with missing or duplicate meta descriptions, or optimizing underperforming pages in search results where CTR improvement is needed.
avoid_when: Pages are dynamically generated at scale where templated descriptions are unavoidable, or when search engines consistently override custom descriptions with their own snippets for the target queries.
source_title: On Page SEO The Definitive Guide FREE Template 2026 Use Unique Relevant Meta Descriptions

name: LocalBusiness Schema Markup Implementation
summary: Step-by-step process to implement LocalBusiness structured data markup for a business with a physical location, ensuring alignment with Google Business Profile data.
use_when: The business has a physical location, has defined opening hours, and has an active Google Business Profile that can be linked to.
avoid_when: The business is purely online with no physical location, lacks opening hours, or does not have a Google Business Profile.
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 LocalBusiness

name: Schema Markup and Emotional Title Tag Optimization for Higher CTR
summary: Implement schema markup types and craft emotionally resonant title tags to earn rich snippets and improve click-through rates from search results.
use_when: When optimizing existing pages or creating new content to improve click-through rates from organic search results, especially for product pages, business listings, tutorials, and review content.
avoid_when: When content does not fit standard schema types (product, organization, how-to, review), or when titles already have strong CTR metrics that risk being disrupted by over-optimization.
source_title: On Page SEO The Definitive Guide FREE Template 2026 Use Schema

name: Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) Optimization Playbook
summary: A step-by-step process to achieve a CLS score of 0.1 or less by preventing unexpected layout shifts during page loading through proper media sizing, ad space reservation, and dynamic content placement.
use_when: When a page has a CLS score above 0.1, when media elements lack explicit dimensions, when ads or dynamic banners are causing content to shift unexpectedly, or when conducting a Core Web Vitals audit.
avoid_when: When the page is fully static with no media, ads, or dynamically injected content, making CLS improvements irrelevant.
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part 3

name: Core Web Vitals LCP Optimization Playbook
summary: A step-by-step process to improve Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) scores to meet Google's 2.5-second threshold, reducing page load friction and supporting page experience ranking signals.
use_when: LCP score is in the 'Needs Improvement' or 'Poor' category as reported by PageSpeed Insights or Google Search Console, or when conducting a site performance audit prior to a content or SEO campaign.
avoid_when: Site already achieves 'Good' LCP scores across all pages, or when performance improvements would require removing above-the-fold content that is critical to user experience or conversion.
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part

name: Schema Markup Implementation for Rich Results
summary: A step-by-step procedure for implementing schema markup types to enable rich results in Google SERPs, improving CTR and conversions through enhanced search result appearances.
use_when: When optimizing a website for search visibility and you want to enable rich results such as star ratings, breadcrumbs, product info, or business details in Google SERPs.
avoid_when: When the site has no local business presence, products, or customer reviews to display — implementing schema without relevant content provides no benefit and may violate Google guidelines.
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Examples of Schema Markup Shown in Google SERPs

name: Implement Schema.org mainEntity and Identifier Structured Data
summary: Step-by-step procedure for correctly implementing Schema.org structured data properties including mainEntity/mainEntityOfPage relationships and identifier usage on web pages.
use_when: When adding structured data markup to a web page to clarify the primary entity of the page and properly represent entity identifiers and relationships.
avoid_when: When a page covers multiple entities of equal importance with no single primary entity — use the 'about' property instead of mainEntity in that case.
source_title: Data model   Schema org Background notes

name: Convert Schema.org Microdata to RDFa Lite Syntax
summary: A step-by-step procedure to convert existing Schema.org Microdata markup into equivalent RDFa Lite syntax by swapping attributes and adding the vocab declaration.
use_when: When you need to implement Schema.org structured data using RDFa Lite instead of Microdata, or when migrating existing Microdata markup to RDFa Lite format.
avoid_when: When using JSON-LD for structured data implementation, as JSON-LD does not require this attribute-swapping process and is a separate syntax entirely.
source_title: Data model   Schema org Mapping to RDFa Lite

name: Broken Link Building Outreach Playbook
summary: A structured process for finding broken links on target pages, recreating the dead content as a superior replacement, and conducting non-pushy outreach to secure backlinks.
use_when: When pursuing link building through broken link replacement, especially when targeting pages with multiple dead links and wanting to maximize outreach conversion rates.
avoid_when: When targeting sites that appear completely abandoned or haven't been updated in years, as response rates are near zero. Also avoid when you cannot create a genuine content replacement for the dead resource.
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 3

name: Enterprise SEO Implementation Playbook
summary: A structured process for implementing SEO at scale on large enterprise websites with thousands or millions of pages, covering site structure, internal linking automation, content scaling, and organizational buy-in.
use_when: Managing SEO for a large enterprise website with thousands or millions of pages, complex site architecture, multiple departments, and significant ecommerce or content footprint (e.g., global brands, large ecommerce sites).
avoid_when: Working on small-to-medium websites with manageable page counts where traditional SEO approaches and manual processes are still feasible.
source_title: Enterprise SEO Strategies Tips for Large Scale Success What Is Enterprise SEO

name: Schema.org Vocabulary Extension Integration Process
summary: Procedure for introducing new experimental terms into Schema.org via the pending status mechanism, followed by evaluation for core incorporation or removal.
use_when: When a new vocabulary term needs to be rapidly introduced into Schema.org for experimental use, especially when the term originates from a hosted extension or external proposal.
avoid_when: When the term is part of a third-party external extension that should remain independently hosted (e.g., gs1.org/voc style broadly applicable external extensions), or when the term is not likely to be consumed by implementers.
source_title: Extending Schemas   Schema org

name: Validate Schema Markup for Google Rich Results Eligibility
summary: A two-stage validation process to check structured data markup against both Schema.org and Google's Rich Results tool to ensure eligibility for rich results display.
use_when: After creating or updating schema markup, before or after publishing, to confirm structured data meets Google's rich results requirements — not just Schema.org standards.
avoid_when: When schema markup types are not supported by Google's rich results documentation, as the tool will not confirm eligibility regardless of markup quality.
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Check Rich Results Eligibility With Google

name: Schema Markup Entity Connection Implementation
summary: Build connected schema markup that creates relationships between entities, enabling Google to understand content context through knowledge graphs.
use_when: When implementing schema markup for important web pages that contain multiple entity types (e.g., Person + Organization, Organization + Service) and you need Google to understand the relationships between those entities.
avoid_when: When a page has only a single entity type with no meaningful relationships to other entities, or when schema markup is already properly connected and validated.
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Are Entities Connected Appropriately

name: Content Gap Analysis Workflow
summary: A systematic process to identify missing or underperforming content opportunities by auditing competitor SERP results and your own site, then improving or creating content that outperforms existing rankings.
use_when: When planning a new content piece for a target keyword, when existing content is underperforming in search rankings, or when conducting a periodic content strategy audit to find new opportunities.
avoid_when: When first-page results are already rock solid with no identifiable gaps, making a 10x improvement implausible, or when the target keyword has such low volume that the investment outweighs the return.
source_title: Content Gap Analysis The Complete Guide

name: Optimize INP and CLS Core Web Vitals
summary: A step-by-step implementation process to achieve optimal Interaction to Next Paint (INP) scores of 200ms or less and maintain stable Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) on pages requiring user interaction.
use_when: Pages have interactive elements requiring fast response times, such as login pages, sign-up pages, e-commerce product pages with add-to-cart buttons, or any page with menus and clickable UI components where INP exceeds 200ms.
avoid_when: Pages are purely static content pages (blog posts, news articles) where the primary user interaction is scrolling, as scrolling is not counted in INP measurement and optimization effort yields minimal benefit.
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part 2

name: Implement Schema Markup for Structured Data SEO
summary: Add structured data markup to webpages using JSON-LD, RDFa, or microdata formats to help search engines explicitly understand content without relying on NLP processing.
use_when: When a webpage, video, or podcast needs to be better understood by Google and Bing, especially when targeting specific queries and high-engagement traffic over generic keyword volume.
avoid_when: When the schema implementation being considered does not provide clear value — not all schema types are beneficial for every content type or page.
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 How Structured Data Helps With Your SEO

name: Duplicate Content Remediation Playbook
summary: A step-by-step process to identify and resolve duplicate content issues on a website to improve search engine ranking for all pages.
use_when: When a site has multiple pages with highly similar or identical content, WordPress tag/category pages creating duplication, or location pages with identical content except the address — and you want every page to rank in search engines.
avoid_when: When the site has no duplicate content issues or when all duplicate pages have already been handled with canonical tags, redirects, or deletions.
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 2

name: Schema Markup Detection and Validation Workflow
summary: A multi-method process to detect, inspect, and validate schema markup on web pages using a Chrome extension, Schema.org validator, and Semrush Site Audit.
use_when: When auditing a website or individual page to verify whether structured data is implemented, correctly typed, and valid in the eyes of search engines.
avoid_when: When a Semrush Site Audit has not yet been completed for the target site, as the Site Audit step depends on prior audit execution.
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 How to Know if a Page or Website Has Schema Mark

name: Implement Schema.org Structured Data Model
summary: A procedure for implementing Schema.org's RDF-based data model with proper type hierarchies, property domains, and ranges for structured data markup.
use_when: When adding structured data markup to web pages for search engine optimization using Schema.org vocabulary, or when extending Schema.org with custom types and properties.
avoid_when: When attempting to build a universal or global ontology of the world, or when a domain-specific ontology with strict semantic precision is required rather than a search-engine-oriented markup system.
source_title: Data model   Schema org

name: Duplicate Content Audit and Remediation
summary: A step-by-step process for identifying duplicate content on a site, diagnosing automatically generated pages, and applying 301 redirects to consolidate indexing signals to the original pages.
use_when: When a site's indexed page count in Google Search Console significantly exceeds the number of manually created pages, or when an ecommerce site has product variation pages, search result pages being indexed, or multiple domain versions (WWW/non-WWW, HTTP/HTTPS) all resolving without redirects.
avoid_when: When all pages are intentionally distinct and indexed page counts match manually created pages, or when canonical tags are already in place and handling duplication — adding 301 redirects on top may conflict with existing consolidation signals.
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Implement Schema.org Structured Data Markup
summary: A procedure for adding schema.org structured data markup to web pages to help search engines understand content and surface it more prominently in search results.
use_when: When adding or upgrading structured data markup on a website to improve search engine understanding and visibility, or when migrating from legacy formats like microformats, RDFa, or data-vocabulary.org to a cross-company standard.
avoid_when: When relying on automated data extraction as a substitute for markup, or when expecting every schema.org information type to appear as rich results in search engine SERPs.
source_title: FAQ   schema org 2

name: Duplicate Content Audit and Remediation Playbook
summary: Identify and resolve duplicate content issues on a website to protect search engine rankings, preserve crawl budget, and ensure distinct pages are indexed by Google.
use_when: When a website has multiple pages with similar or identical content, when organic traffic is declining without a clear cause, when an ecommerce or large site may be wasting crawl budget on near-duplicate pages, or when content has been scraped or copied from other sources.
avoid_when: When all site pages already contain fully distinct and original information and no duplication signals have been observed in search performance data.
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide What Is Duplicate Content

name: Schema.org Structured Data Implementation Playbook
summary: A step-by-step procedure for correctly implementing schema.org structured data markup on web pages to maximize search engine understanding and utility.
use_when: When adding schema.org structured data markup to a website for the first time, or when auditing/improving existing markup for search engine visibility.
avoid_when: When content is hidden from users via hidden divs or other hidden page elements — do not mark up content that is not visible to page visitors.
source_title: FAQ   schema org 3

name: Schema Markup Implementation via CMS or GTM
summary: A step-by-step procedure for injecting JSON-LD schema markup onto a website using either a CMS-native method or Google Tag Manager, while avoiding duplicate schema generation.
use_when: You have generated JSON-LD schema markup and need to deploy it on a website, regardless of whether you have direct backend access or are working on a client site.
avoid_when: The website already has automated schema markup generation active and you cannot disable it before implementing a bespoke method, as this will cause duplication.
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 How to Get Schema Markup Onto a Website

name: Featured Snippet Capture Playbook (Position Zero Optimization)
summary: A structured process for identifying and optimizing content to capture Google's Featured Snippet (position zero) results, covering opportunity discovery, snippet type identification, and format-specific content optimization.
use_when: When a content creator or SEO professional wants to rank in Featured Snippet positions for target keywords, especially when SERPs already show existing Featured Snippets for those terms.
avoid_when: When targeting keywords that do not yet have Featured Snippets in SERPs, or when the content type (e.g., transactional/commercial pages) is unlikely to match Google's snippet intent.
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part

name: GEO Content Extractability Optimization
summary: Structure content so individual paragraphs can be retrieved and used accurately by AI systems without requiring surrounding context.
use_when: Writing or auditing paragraphs that contain definitions, explanations, comparisons, or key facts intended to be surfaced in AI-generated answers.
avoid_when: Writing narrative or storytelling content where context dependency is intentional and AI retrieval is not a goal.
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 3 Content must be easy to extract and reus

name: Access Machine-Readable Schema.org Vocabulary Definitions
summary: Step-by-step procedure for developers to retrieve machine-readable representations of Schema.org schemas in various formats for use in structured data applications.
use_when: When building or integrating applications that consume Schema.org structured data and require programmatic access to vocabulary definitions, term metadata, or context files.
avoid_when: When seeking authoritative OWL definitions for Schema.org terms, as the OWL mapping is explicitly noted as an approximation and not authoritative.
source_title: Developers   Schema org

name: Featured Snippet (Position Zero) Content Optimization Playbook
summary: Structure and format content to capture Google's featured snippet placements (position zero) for long-tail keywords and voice search queries.
use_when: When creating or updating content that targets informational queries, how-to questions, or comparison topics where featured snippets are likely to appear in SERPs.
avoid_when: When targeting purely transactional or navigational queries where featured snippets rarely appear, or when content is not structured around answerable questions.
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part 2

name: Optimize Content for Featured Snippet Capture
summary: Structure content to match the appropriate featured snippet type based on query intent, targeting position zero in Google search results.
use_when: Creating or optimizing content that targets informational queries with clear intent (definitions, how-to steps, comparisons, or lists) where a featured snippet could appear.
avoid_when: Targeting transactional or navigational queries where featured snippets rarely appear, or when the content topic does not have a clear query intent match to one of the four snippet types.
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google Types of Featured Snippets

name: Schema Markup Implementation for Business Entities
summary: Implement JSON-LD schema markup for Organization and Person entities on a business website to improve click-through rates from Google search results.
use_when: A business or individual wants to enhance their search presence by adding structured data markup to their website, specifically to describe their organization and key people using schema.org vocabulary.
avoid_when: The website already has validated Organization and Person schema markup in place, or when the site is not indexed by Google and structured data would have no impact.
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 It s Time to Write Your Own Schema Markup

name: Featured Snippet (Position Zero) Optimization Playbook
summary: A structured process for optimizing existing first-page-ranking content to capture Google featured snippets by formatting content in ways Google prefers to feature.
use_when: Your page already ranks on Google's first page for a target keyword and you want to capture the featured snippet (position zero) placement above traditional results.
avoid_when: Your page does not yet rank on Google's first page — featured snippet optimization will have near-zero impact on pages ranking beyond page 1 regardless of formatting quality.
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part 3

name: Implement Organization and Product Schema Markup
summary: Step-by-step process for adding Organization and Product schema markup to establish entity relationships, validate business identity, and enable rich search results.
use_when: When building or auditing structured data for a business website, especially when the site has an About page, product/service pages, or an ecommerce catalog requiring entity recognition by search engines.

name: Implement Microdata Markup on Web Content
summary: A step-by-step procedure for adding semantic microdata markup to HTML content using schema.org vocabulary so that search engines can better understand and display structured information.
use_when: When adding structured data to a webpage to help search engines understand specific entities such as movies, people, places, or videos using HTML5 microdata attributes.
avoid_when: When using JSON-LD or RDFa as the preferred structured data format, or when the page content does not represent a discrete schema.org entity type.
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 1 How to mark up your content using microdata

name: Mark Up Hidden/Implicit Information Using Schema.org Meta Tags
summary: Use meta tags with content attributes to add Schema.org structured data for information that cannot be directly marked up because it appears in images, Flash objects, or is implied rather than explicitly stated in HTML.
use_when: Information valuable for structured data markup is conveyed through images (e.g., star rating graphics), Flash objects (e.g., video duration), or is implied by context (e.g., currency), making direct HTML markup impossible.
avoid_when: The information can be marked up directly using standard HTML elements — meta with content should not be used as a shortcut for information that is already present in accessible text form.
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 3c Missing implicit information use the meta tag with content

name: Implement Schema.org Enumerations and Canonical References with Microdata
summary: Replace plain text property values with Schema.org enumeration URLs and add canonical reference links using the appropriate HTML elements for invisible or visible linking.
use_when: When marking up structured data with Microdata and a property accepts a limited set of values (enumerations) or when linking to third-party pages that describe the same item to improve search engine understanding.
avoid_when: When a property does not have a corresponding Schema.org enumeration specification, or when the property accepts free-form text values rather than a constrained set of options.
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 3b Enumerations and canonical references use link with href

name: Implement Schema.org Structured Data Markup on Web Pages
summary: Add schema.org structured data markup to web pages so search engines can better understand page content and deliver richer search results.
use_when: When adding structured data to a website to improve search engine understanding, enable rich results, or provide detailed entity information beyond what Facebook Open Graph offers.
avoid_when: When the site already uses a conflicting non-schema.org vocabulary and a full migration is not feasible, or when the page has no identifiable entities or structured content to mark up.

name: Implement Schema.org Structured Data Markup
summary: A procedure for adding schema.org vocabulary to web content using Microdata, RDFa, or JSON-LD formats so search engines can better understand and display page content.
