name: Implement FAQPage Schema for AI Search Optimization
summary: A structured approach to implementing FAQPage schema markup as part of an AI-search-aware SEO strategy, recognizing that AI engines heavily cite FAQ content even when Google limits FAQ rich snippets.
use_when: When optimizing a site for visibility in AI search engines (e.g., ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini), when building a business case for structured data implementation beyond traditional Google rich results, or when auditing structured data strategy to reflect evolving search landscape.
avoid_when: When the sole goal is improving Google rich result appearances, as Google has limited FAQ snippet display. Also avoid over-investing with explosive growth expectations — gains are likely to be incremental.
source_title: SEO in 2026 Higher standards AI influence and a web still catching up FAQ growth

name: AI SEO Team Transition Playbook
summary: A structured process for expanding an existing SEO team's capabilities to cover AI search visibility, including content structuring, technical priorities, and performance measurement across AI platforms.
use_when: An organization has an existing SEO team skilled in traditional SEO and needs to extend their capabilities to achieve brand visibility in AI-generated answers and citations.
avoid_when: Building an SEO team from scratch with no existing traditional SEO foundation, or when the organization lacks resources for a 4-12 month transition timeline.
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Skills Gap Between Traditional and AI

name: AI-Ready SEO Team Scale Phase: Institutionalizing AI-First Operations
summary: A five-step process to permanently embed AI-first SEO thinking into team structure, roles, workflows, training, and leadership reporting so AI SEO capability scales without constant resets.
use_when: Your team has completed earlier AI SEO pilot phases and needs to formalize roles, expand proven approaches across full programs, and make AI-first execution the organizational default over the next six months.
avoid_when: Your team has not yet run AI SEO pilots or validated any frameworks — this phase assumes prior experimentation and proven results exist to scale from.
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Phase 3 Scale

name: Internal Linking Authority Flow Playbook
summary: A shortcut method for distributing link authority to new or low-authority pages by linking from established older pages using keyword-rich anchor text.
use_when: When publishing new pages that lack inbound links or link authority and need a ranking boost from existing site equity.
avoid_when: When the site is new with no established older pages to link from, or when over-analyzing link authority flow would delay implementation.
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 5

name: LLMs.txt Implementation via SEO Plugin
summary: Enable llms.txt functionality through an SEO plugin to guide LLM crawlbot behavior and improve content retrieval for AI-generated answers.
use_when: Your site is developing an AI search strategy and your SEO plugin (e.g., All in One SEO, Yoast, Rank Math) offers llms.txt functionality.
avoid_when: There is no SEO plugin available with llms.txt support, or your AI search strategy has not yet been defined and you risk enabling it as a default without strategic intent.
source_title: SEO in 2026 Higher standards AI influence and a web still catching up LLMs txt

name: UX and Site Speed Optimization for SEO Rankings
summary: A step-by-step implementation process to improve site loading speed and user experience signals that directly and indirectly affect SEO rankings.
use_when: Your site has high bounce rates, slow load times, poor PageSpeed scores, or you suspect poor UX is harming search rankings or reducing link acquisition.
avoid_when: Your site already scores highly on PageSpeed Insights and has low bounce rates with strong engagement metrics — further optimization may yield diminishing returns or introduce UX tradeoffs (e.g., lazy loading disrupting above-the-fold experience).
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 3

name: Internal Linking Authority Distribution Playbook
summary: A simple internal linking strategy that routes link authority from established older pages to newer, low-authority pages to accelerate their ranking potential.
use_when: When publishing new pages that lack backlinks or internal links and need an authority boost to become competitive in search results.
avoid_when: When the old page's content is completely unrelated to the new page — forced internal links on irrelevant content can harm user experience and signal quality.
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 5

name: AI Search Visibility Tracking Implementation
summary: A structured procedure for monitoring your brand's performance in AI-generated search responses across platforms like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews, using specialized tools or manual methods.
use_when: Core AI SEO structure is already in place and you need to measure brand visibility, citation frequency, and mention sentiment across AI search platforms — not traditional rankings or organic traffic.
avoid_when: You are still building foundational AI SEO structure; entity database registration specifically should be skipped until core AI SEO is established.
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Tracking AI Performance

name: SEO On-Site Optimization Implementation Playbook
summary: A step-by-step procedure for implementing foundational SEO best practices on a website to improve search engine rankings and organic traffic through content optimization, technical improvements, and authority building.
use_when: When launching a new website, conducting an SEO audit, or improving rankings for an existing site that lacks foundational on-page optimization.
avoid_when: When the site has already implemented all foundational SEO elements and requires advanced technical SEO or link-building-only strategies rather than on-site basics.
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026

name: Linkable Content Creation Playbook
summary: A structured approach to creating content specifically designed to attract natural backlinks, based on analysis of 900+ million blog posts identifying formats and elements that generate more linking opportunities.
use_when: When building a link acquisition strategy through content creation, particularly when trying to earn natural backlinks rather than pursuing outreach-heavy link building campaigns.
avoid_when: When the goal is purely traffic or conversion optimization without a link-building component, or when the site lacks the authority or audience to attract organic links regardless of content format.
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 6

name: Schema.org Structured Data Markup Implementation
summary: A step-by-step procedure for webmasters to correctly implement schema.org structured data markup on web pages, ensuring search engine compatibility and maximum content utility.
use_when: When adding structured data markup to a web page for the first time, or auditing existing markup for compliance with schema.org best practices.
avoid_when: When content is hidden from users (e.g., in hidden divs or non-visible page elements), as marking up hidden content violates schema.org guidelines.
source_title: FAQ   schema org 3

name: AI-Era SEO Content Optimization Playbook
summary: Structure and optimize content to maintain visibility in both traditional search engines and AI-driven search tools by combining keyword fundamentals with credibility-building and content organization best practices.
use_when: When creating or auditing content that needs to perform in both Google Search and AI-generated answer environments (e.g., ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity).
avoid_when: When targeting purely paid search or social media channels where on-page structure and organic SEO signals are not ranking factors.
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 6

name: AI-Era SEO Content Strategy: Human Expertise + AI Efficiency
summary: A procedure for adapting SEO content creation to the AI-saturated web by combining genuine human expertise with AI tooling to stand out from generic AI-generated content floods.
use_when: When developing or auditing a content strategy in an environment where AI-generated content is pervasive and organic search performance is declining due to undifferentiated content
avoid_when: When building a purely automated content pipeline without human expertise input, or when the goal is volume-based content production without differentiation
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 5

name: Build Brand Authority for AI Citation Visibility
summary: A structured process to increase the likelihood of being cited by AI systems like ChatGPT by building the three key brand authority signals: branded web mentions, branded anchors, and branded search volume.
use_when: When a brand wants to appear in AI-generated responses, AI Overviews, or ChatGPT citations, and currently lacks measurable branded web presence or low domain authority.
avoid_when: When the brand is already a top-cited authority (Domain Rating 80+) and AI citation presence is already established — effort is better directed at content depth or other SEO fundamentals.
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 7

name: Develop How-To and Explainer Content for Generative Engine Optimization (GEO)
summary: Create how-to and explainer content assets optimized for 2026-era search engines that answer questions directly rather than referring traffic, with particular focus on technology companies targeting users in research and consideration phases.
use_when: Planning 2025-2026 content strategy for technology companies, or when organic search traffic is declining due to AI-answered SERPs absorbing queries that previously drove clicks.
avoid_when: When targeting purely transactional bottom-funnel queries where product pages or landing pages are more appropriate than educational content.
source_title: Seer Interactive Blog

name: Bounce Rate Reduction Playbook
summary: A structured procedure to diagnose and reduce harmful bounce rates by aligning page content with user intent, improving design trust signals, and optimizing navigation.
use_when: Pages show high bounce rates that correlate with poor engagement metrics, low conversions, or mismatched ad/organic traffic destinations.
avoid_when: Pages are intentionally single-purpose (e.g., contact confirmation pages, recipe cards, or reference lookups) where a bounce signals successful task completion rather than failure.
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Why Do People Bounce

name: Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) Adaptation Playbook
summary: Reorient SEO strategy to remain visible and cited in AI-generated search answers while shifting content and conversion focus toward transactional queries that still drive clicks.
use_when: When organic traffic is declining due to AI Overviews or AI Mode capturing informational query traffic, or when planning a forward-looking SEO strategy in a zero-click search environment.
avoid_when: When the site operates exclusively in a niche where AI Overviews are rarely triggered (e.g., highly specialized B2B technical queries), or when traffic volume rather than conversion quality is the primary KPI.
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 2

name: Bounce Rate Diagnosis and Improvement Playbook
summary: A procedure for assessing whether a site's bounce rate is problematic and identifying the root causes to address, using industry benchmarks and traffic source analysis.
use_when: When a site's bounce rate appears high and you need to determine if it is genuinely problematic and what is causing it, or when optimizing for conversion and engagement improvements.
avoid_when: When the site is a blog or single-page content destination where high bounce rates are expected and normal (up to 90%), or when traffic is primarily from display ads or social media where high bounce rates are inherent to the channel.
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It

name: SEO Hygiene Modernization for AI-Influenced Search (2026)
summary: A strategic checklist for site owners to align their SEO practices with AI search influence, covering robots.txt as a policy surface, LLMs.txt adoption, and FAQ structured content implementation.
use_when: When auditing or updating an existing site's SEO posture to account for AI-driven search engines and LLM-based content extraction in 2025-2026 environments.
avoid_when: When performing emergency technical SEO fixes or when the site has not yet established basic crawlability and indexing — foundational issues must be resolved before these advanced signals.
source_title: SEO in 2026 Higher standards AI influence and a web still catching up Not a rewrite A new layer on t

name: Broken Link Building Outreach Playbook
summary: Build backlinks by identifying broken links (404 pages) on target websites and offering your working content as a replacement, using a help-first outreach approach.
use_when: You want to build high-quality backlinks to your site and have relevant content that can serve as a replacement for broken links on authoritative external websites.
avoid_when: You do not have relevant replacement content to offer, or your outreach would only request a link without providing genuine value to the target site owner.
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide What Is Broken Link Building

name: Broken Link Building Outreach Workflow
summary: A step-by-step process for identifying broken links on target pages, creating replacement content, and conducting outreach to secure backlinks by offering genuine value to site owners.
use_when: When executing a broken link building campaign to acquire quality backlinks, particularly when targeting active sites where conversion rate matters and you have capacity to create replacement content.
avoid_when: When targeting sites that appear abandoned or haven't been updated in years, or when you cannot invest the time to create original replacement content (e.g., if you would need to copy dead content verbatim).
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 3

name: AI Crawlbot Management Strategy via Robots.txt
summary: A process for websites to strategically assess and update their robots.txt and broader bot management approach to handle the surge in LLM crawlbots like GPTBot and ClaudeBot.
use_when: LLM bot traffic is increasing significantly on your site, you have no existing AI crawlbot policy, or your current bot management strategy predates the rise of AI crawlers and needs updating.
avoid_when: Your site has no meaningful content to protect or monetize, or you are operating a site explicitly designed to be crawled by all bots without restriction.
source_title: SEO in 2026 Higher standards AI influence and a web still catching up Robots txt as a bouncer

name: Social Capital Arbitrage: Cross-Platform Content Redistribution
summary: A procedure for redistributing content across social networks to accumulate status, while managing attribution norms to avoid backlash from original creators and audiences.
use_when: When a user or account seeks to grow status on a social network by reposting content that performed well on a different platform with a different audience or graph configuration.
avoid_when: When the target audience significantly overlaps with the source platform's audience, reducing the arbitrage opportunity, or when the content type (e.g., long-form video) does not translate well to the destination platform's format.
source_title: Status as a Service StaaS Social Capital Arbitrage

name: Duplicate Content Prevention and Remediation Playbook
summary: A procedure for identifying and resolving duplicate content issues on a website to protect search engine rankings, crawl budget, and organic traffic.
use_when: When a site has multiple pages with similar or identical content, when launching new pages that may overlap with existing content, or when diagnosing ranking or indexation problems on ecommerce or large-scale sites.
avoid_when: When content similarity is minimal and pages clearly serve distinct informational purposes with no observable ranking or indexation issues.
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide What Is Duplicate Content

name: Duplicate Content Audit and Remediation Playbook
summary: A step-by-step process to identify, investigate, and fix duplicate content issues on a website using Google Search Console and 301 redirects.
use_when: When a site has unexpectedly high indexed page counts, ecommerce product variations creating duplicate URLs, search result pages getting indexed, or when rankings are dropping due to suspected duplicate content issues.
avoid_when: When the site is newly launched and has not yet been crawled, or when duplicate content is intentional syndication with proper canonical tags already in place.
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Build AI-Compatible Product Feeds for Performance Max and Smart Bidding
summary: Construct product feeds that are structured to work effectively with Performance Max campaigns, AI-driven bidding systems, and generative search engines rather than relying on simple product listings.
use_when: Setting up or auditing product feeds for e-commerce campaigns that use Performance Max, smart bidding, or need visibility in AI-generated search results.
avoid_when: Running simple manual CPC campaigns with no AI bidding components where feed optimization for ML systems is not relevant.
source_title: Seer Interactive Blog

name: Featured Snippet Type Optimization Playbook
summary: Structure content to match the correct featured snippet format based on query intent, targeting Google's position zero by aligning content type with the four main snippet categories.
use_when: Creating or optimizing content that targets informational queries where featured snippet capture is a goal, especially 'what is', how-to, ranked list, or comparative data queries.
avoid_when: Targeting navigational or transactional queries where featured snippets rarely appear, or when content cannot be restructured to match a specific snippet format.
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google Types of Featured Snippets

name: SEO Fundamentals for Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) Readiness
summary: Configure technical SEO and content quality signals so that AI systems can reliably retrieve, interpret, and attribute your content when assembling AI-generated answers.
use_when: When preparing a website or content asset to be surfaced, referenced, or cited by AI-powered search engines and generative answer systems (e.g., ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews).
avoid_when: When optimizing exclusively for traditional search ranking signals where AI retrieval and attribution are not a concern, or when the site is intentionally gated from AI crawlers.
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 1 SEO fundamentals are the foundation

name: 2026 Technical SEO Fundamentals Implementation Checklist
summary: Implement core technical SEO requirements and avoid common errors based on 2025 Web Almanac industry benchmarks, covering HTTPS, metadata, and crawlability standards.
use_when: Auditing or building a website for SEO compliance in 2025-2026, particularly when benchmarking against current industry adoption rates for core technical elements.
avoid_when: When performing advanced AI-driven SEO or structured data optimization — this playbook covers foundational technical SEO only and does not address emerging LLMs.txt or structured data complexities.
source_title: SEO in 2026 Higher standards AI influence and a web still catching up Technical SEO is getting easie

name: SEO Defaults Implementation via CMS and SEO Tools
summary: A foundational technical SEO setup procedure using CMS platforms and SEO tools to implement the core default elements that form industry-standard best practices for search visibility and structured data retrieval.
use_when: When setting up a new website on a CMS, auditing an existing site for missing technical SEO fundamentals, or aligning a site with emerging SEO standards heading into 2026 and beyond.
avoid_when: When the site has a fully custom build without CMS or plugin support, or when advanced technical configurations have already been verified and validated — do not assume this checklist replaces a full audit.
source_title: SEO in 2026 Higher standards AI influence and a web still catching up The cementing of SEO defaults

name: Date Schema Implementation for Freshness SEO Boost
summary: A technical SEO procedure for updating date schema markup and visible date signals to leverage content freshness as a ranking factor, targeting competitors who fake freshness via title tag dates without actual content updates.
use_when: Targeting keywords where freshness is a ranking factor and competitors are adding current dates to title tags without updating underlying HTML or schema markup, creating a technical SEO gap to exploit.
avoid_when: Content types where freshness is not a ranking signal, or when the page content has not actually been meaningfully updated and only cosmetic date changes are being made.
source_title: Technical SEO The Ultimate Guide for 2026 Case Study 2

name: Mobile Usability Audit with Lighthouse
summary: Use Google Lighthouse to proactively identify and fix mobile usability issues before users encounter them, covering problems like small text, overlapping buttons, and viewport overflow.
use_when: When conducting routine site maintenance, after deploying site changes, or when user complaints about mobile experience begin to surface
avoid_when: When the site has no mobile presence or is exclusively accessed via desktop-only internal tools
source_title: Technical SEO The Ultimate Guide for 2026 Check for Mobile Usability Issues

name: Noindex Duplicate Content Pages Using GSC Verification
summary: Add noindex tags to duplicate content pages and verify proper implementation using Google Search Console's URL Inspection tool.
use_when: Duplicate content pages are being indexed by search engines and causing content duplication issues that could harm SEO performance.
avoid_when: Pages have unique content that should remain indexed, or when you want to block crawling entirely rather than just indexing (use robots.txt instead).
source_title: Technical SEO The Ultimate Guide for 2026 Noindex Pages That Don t Have Unique Content

name: Internal Linking Audit and Optimization for Organic Traffic Growth
summary: A structured process to identify and fix internal linking gaps by connecting high-priority content, using keyword-rich anchor text, and linking content to product/service pages to unlock stagnant organic traffic.
use_when: Site has existing high-quality content that is not ranking or growing in traffic despite 2+ months of publication, and an audit reveals missing or weak internal links between content and key pages.
avoid_when: Site lacks sufficient high-quality content to link between, as internal linking optimization cannot compensate for thin or low-quality content.
source_title: Technical SEO The Ultimate Guide for 2026 Case Study 1

name: Content Extractability Optimization for AI Retrieval
summary: Structure content so individual paragraphs can be accurately retrieved and used by AI systems without requiring surrounding context, increasing the likelihood your expertise surfaces in AI-generated answers.
use_when: Writing or auditing paragraphs that contain definitions, explanations, comparisons, or key facts intended to appear in AI-generated responses
avoid_when: Writing narrative or storytelling content where sequential context is intentionally required, or paragraphs that are purely transitional with no standalone informational value
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 3 Content must be easy to extract and reus

name: Flat Site Structure Implementation Playbook
summary: Organize and audit your website's architecture to ensure a flat, crawlable structure with no orphan pages, consistent URL patterns, and breadcrumb navigation.
use_when: Building or auditing a website with many pages (especially ecommerce sites with large product catalogs) where crawlability, internal linking, and page discoverability are concerns.
avoid_when: Managing a small blog or simple local business website where URL structure complexity and deep auditing are unnecessary overhead.
source_title: Technical SEO The Ultimate Guide for 2026 Use a Flat Organized Site Structure

name: GEO Content Structuring for AI Answer Visibility
summary: A step-by-step process to structure content so it is retrieved, cited, and recommended by generative AI systems through clarity, authority, and self-contained passage design.
use_when: When optimizing content to appear in AI-generated answers across systems like ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Google AI Overviews, especially when building long-term visibility beyond traditional search rankings.
avoid_when: When seeking short-term traffic wins or when the content strategy is purely click-based; also avoid when brand recognition alone is expected to drive AI citations without structural content investment.
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions Frequently asked questions

name: Brand Visibility Optimization for LLM-Powered Search (GEO)
summary: A procedure for optimizing brand authority and entity positioning within AI/LLM-powered search experiences by leveraging unlinked mentions across trusted sources rather than relying solely on traditional backlink strategies.
use_when: When optimizing for AI-powered search engines, LLM-based answer engines, or generative search experiences where brand entity recognition and cosine similarity across trusted sources matter more than backlink profiles.
avoid_when: When the sole goal is improving rankings in traditional search engines where dofollow backlinks remain the primary authority signal and unlinked mentions have minimal impact.
source_title: GEO LLMO AEO It s All Just SEO 2

name: SparkToro Audience Research and Marketing Intelligence Workflow
summary: Use SparkToro to research audience behavior across search engines and AI tools, uncover influence patterns, and develop messaging strategies based on where audiences consume content before they search.
use_when: When a marketer needs to understand where a target audience spends time online, which search or AI tools they use, and what content influences their behavior prior to a search or purchase decision.
avoid_when: When direct attribution data is the primary goal — SparkToro reveals influence and behavioral patterns, not last-click conversion paths. Also avoid relying solely on this data when direct traffic analytics show anomalous attribution patterns (~45% direct) that may skew interpretation.
source_title: SparkToro Blog

name: Post-Migration Technical SEO Recovery Playbook
summary: A systematic audit and fix procedure to recover organic traffic lost after a website migration, addressing orphan pages, redirect chains, canonicalization errors, broken links, and sitemap issues.
use_when: Organic traffic drops significantly following a website migration, URL restructuring, or site rebuild — particularly when traffic loss occurs within weeks to months of the migration event.
avoid_when: The traffic drop is unrelated to a migration (e.g., algorithm update or seasonal shift), or when the site has not undergone any structural or URL changes recently.
source_title: Technical SEO The Ultimate Guide for 2026 Case Study 3

name: GSC Inspect Feature: Diagnose Indexing and Content Quality Issues
summary: Use Google Search Console's Inspect feature to diagnose why pages aren't indexed, verify Google's rendering of pages, and identify duplicate or thin content issues created by CMS systems or other sources.
use_when: Pages on your site are not getting indexed, you suspect Google cannot access all page content, or your CMS may be generating duplicate URLs for the same content.
avoid_when: All pages are confirmed indexed and rendering correctly with no ranking anomalies or duplicate content concerns present.
source_title: Technical SEO The Ultimate Guide for 2026 GSC Inspect

name: Quarterly SEO Technical Audit and Fix Procedure
summary: A repeatable quarterly procedure to identify and fix technical SEO issues including broken links, XML sitemap errors, CDN setup, hreflang tags, and structured data implementation.
use_when: When conducting quarterly SEO audits, onboarding a new website for SEO management, or after major site migrations where technical issues may have been introduced.
avoid_when: When the site has just undergone a full audit within the last 4-6 weeks and no major changes have been deployed since.
source_title: Technical SEO The Ultimate Guide for 2026 Reduce Web Page Size

name: Spot and Resolve Page Indexing Issues
summary: A systematic procedure to identify pages that search engine spiders cannot crawl or index, fix coverage issues, and ensure deep pages are discoverable through internal links and sitemaps.
use_when: When a site has pages that are not appearing in Google search results, when deep pages several links from the homepage are suspected to have crawling problems, or when conducting a technical SEO audit for sites with 10k+ pages.
avoid_when: When the primary concern is homepage indexing — homepage indexing issues are uncommon and this workflow is optimized for deep page problems.
source_title: Technical SEO The Ultimate Guide for 2026 Spot Indexing Issues

name: Implement Canonical URLs for Product Variations
summary: Designate a primary product page and use canonical tags to consolidate duplicate or near-duplicate URLs caused by product variations such as size, color, or other attributes.
use_when: An ecommerce site has multiple URLs generated by product variations (e.g., size, color, style) that result in near-duplicate pages without canonical designation.
avoid_when: Pages have substantially different content and are not variations of the same product, making each URL genuinely unique and independently valuable.
source_title: Technical SEO The Ultimate Guide for 2026 Use Canonical URLs

name: Link Roundup Outreach Playbook
summary: Find relevant link roundup posts in your niche and pitch your linkable asset for inclusion to earn backlinks and potential social shares.
use_when: You have a high-quality linkable asset (blog post, video, tool, quiz, survey) published and want to earn backlinks through outreach to curated roundup posts.
avoid_when: Your content is thin, low-quality, or not yet published — you need something genuinely worth linking to before pitching.
source_title: What Are Backlinks in SEO Why You Need Them Best Practices Part 1

name: LLM Content Optimization for AI Crawlers
summary: Structure and annotate content so AI crawlers and LLMs can correctly interpret isolated chunks, improving QA accuracy and brand visibility in AI-generated responses.
use_when: Publishing or updating web content, documentation, knowledgebases, public repositories, or code samples that AI crawlers may index and LLMs may cite or reference.
avoid_when: Content is delivered primarily via client-side JavaScript rendering with no server-side fallback, as most AI crawlers will not see the content at all until rendering support improves.
source_title: GEO LLMO AEO It s All Just SEO 4

name: Duplicate Content Audit and Authorship Verification
summary: Scan your website for duplicate or thin content, check uniqueness across the web, and verify Google recognizes your site as the original author of your content.
use_when: You suspect your site has duplicate or thin content issues, you want to confirm your content is unique across the web, or you need to verify that Google attributes original authorship to your pages.
avoid_when: Other sites are copying your content — that is their duplicate content problem, not yours, and this workflow does not address incoming content theft.
source_title: Technical SEO The Ultimate Guide for 2026 Use an SEO Audit Tool to Find Duplicate Content

name: AI Search Visibility Optimization for Brands
summary: A structured process for brands to achieve visibility in AI-powered search results by combining competitor analysis, Reddit community strategy, LLM grounding optimization, and human-first digital PR.
use_when: A brand needs to improve visibility in AI search results (LLMs, AI Overviews) and traditional keyword-based SEO strategies are underperforming or insufficient for capturing AI-generated responses.
avoid_when: Brand is in early-stage content development without existing authority signals, or when budget cannot support digital PR outreach and ongoing prompt tracking; also avoid when content volume goals override quality and brand affinity maintenance.
source_title: The Moz Blog Expert SEO Inbound Marketing Advice   Moz

name: SEO-Friendly Website Architecture Setup
summary: Structure and interlink website pages so that search engine crawlers can discover 100% of pages and link authority flows appropriately to high-priority pages.
use_when: Setting up a new website or auditing an existing site's internal linking structure to improve crawlability, indexation, and PageRank distribution.
avoid_when: Site is a single-page application or intentionally has pages that should not be indexed or linked (e.g., private admin pages, staging environments).
source_title: Website Architecture How to Setup an SEO Friendly Structure What Is Website Architecture

name: Moving Man Method for Backlink Acquisition
summary: Identify outdated, rebranded, or defunct resources that still have sites linking to them, then contact those linking sites to suggest your up-to-date content as a replacement.
use_when: You have a linkable asset that serves as a replacement for an outdated, rebranded, or defunct resource that other websites are still linking to.
avoid_when: You do not yet have a relevant linkable asset to offer as a replacement, or the outdated resource is still actively maintained and accurate.
source_title: What Are Backlinks in SEO Why You Need Them Best Practices Part 1

name: Guest Blogging Prospecting via Reverse Image Search
summary: Identify quality guest posting opportunities in your niche by finding sites where prolific guest authors in your industry have already been published, using reverse image search on their author bio headshots.
use_when: You are starting link building through guest posting and need to find quality, niche-relevant sites that already accept guest contributors.
avoid_when: You are targeting sites outside your industry niche, or when the identified sites appear low-quality or spammy.
source_title: What Are Backlinks in SEO Why You Need Them Best Practices Part 3

name: Outsourcing Decision and Implementation for AI-Ready SEO Teams
summary: A structured process for identifying when to outsource SEO capabilities versus building in-house, and how to engage external specialists effectively for AI-first SEO needs.
use_when: Your team has identified specific capability gaps (e.g., AI visibility tracking, passage optimization, Reddit brand presence) that are either highly specialized or short-term in nature, and internal hiring would slow down execution.
avoid_when: Outsourcing is being considered as a default fix for general underperformance, or when the skill in question is becoming core to long-term strategy and should be internalized instead.
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 3 When to Borrow Outsource or Consult

name: Build AI-Ready SEO Skills Internally via Team Upskilling
summary: A structured internal training process to develop AI SEO capabilities within an existing team by building on current skills, distributing knowledge across roles, and iterating on content using AI citation patterns.
use_when: Your team has strong existing SEO foundations, you have a limited budget for new hires, and you want to develop AI-ready SEO capabilities without bringing in external resources.
avoid_when: You need fast output results (within weeks), your team lacks foundational SEO knowledge, or skill concentration risk is already present with one person owning multiple new disciplines.
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 1 When to Build Develop Internally

name: Technical SEO Implementation Checklist
summary: A structured process to ensure a website meets technical search engine requirements for improved organic rankings, covering crawlability, indexability, rendering, and site architecture.
use_when: When launching a new website, conducting a technical SEO audit, or when a site with strong content is underperforming in organic search due to suspected technical barriers.
avoid_when: When the site already ranks well and technical changes risk destabilizing existing performance; minor technical imperfections do not need to block publishing or ranking efforts.
source_title: Technical SEO The Ultimate Guide for 2026 What Is Technical SEO

name: SEO-Friendly Flat Site Architecture Setup
summary: Establish a flat, hierarchical website structure where any page is reachable within 4 clicks, using category pages, consistent URLs, and HTML navigation to maximize crawlability and link authority flow.
use_when: Setting up a new website from scratch, redesigning an existing site's navigation structure, or managing a site approaching or exceeding 1,000 pages that needs scalable organization.
avoid_when: Site has fewer than 1,000 pages and all content is already well-interlinked without category organization — flat linking may suffice without formal category hierarchy.
source_title: Website Architecture How to Setup an SEO Friendly Structure Best Practices

name: Backlink Acquisition for SEO Rankings
summary: A procedure for obtaining and leveraging backlinks to improve search engine rankings, drive direct traffic, and enhance brand awareness.
use_when: When building or improving a website's SEO strategy and seeking to increase search engine rankings through off-page authority signals.
avoid_when: When the focus is solely on on-page SEO or technical SEO improvements with no off-page link building component required.
source_title: What Are Backlinks in SEO Why You Need Them What Are Backlinks

name: Link Bait Creation and Promotion Playbook
summary: A structured process for creating content designed to attract backlinks by combining emotional triggers, utility, and active promotion — based on research-backed principles of viral content sharing.
use_when: When building link authority in a niche through content, especially when competitor backlink profiles can be analyzed and content promotion channels are available.
avoid_when: When you lack the ability to promote content after publishing, or when operating in a niche where emotional content is inappropriate (e.g., highly regulated industries requiring purely neutral, factual communication).
source_title: What Is Link Bait Here s Everything You Need To Know Best Practices Part 3

name: Crawl Budget Optimization Playbook
summary: A structured process to maximize Googlebot's crawl efficiency on large or complex websites by eliminating waste and prioritizing high-value pages.
use_when: Site has 10,000+ pages, recently added hundreds of important pages, has orphan pages, or has redirect chains that may be limiting indexation and SEO performance.
avoid_when: Site is small (under a few hundred pages) with stable content and no crawl or indexation issues — crawl budget is unlikely to be a limiting factor.
source_title: What is Crawl Budget and Why Does It Matter for SEO

name: Hiring AI-Ready SEO Talent: Buy vs. Build Decision Process
summary: A structured process for identifying, scoping, and recruiting specialized SEO talent with AI-era capabilities when internal upskilling is too slow or insufficient.
use_when: Your team lacks specialized skills in structured data, entity modeling, AI visibility analytics, or AI content strategy, and you need to acquire that expertise faster than internal training allows — typically at mid-sized or enterprise scale with budget flexibility.
avoid_when: You need a short-term fix rather than a long-term strategic capability, or when your team already has the skills in-house and only needs upskilling.
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 2 When to Buy Hire New Talent

name: Moving Man Method for Backlink Acquisition
summary: Identify shut-down or removed content that others were linking to, then contact those linking sites to offer your content as a replacement.
use_when: You have content that is relevant to a website or resource that has recently shut down, rebranded, or removed content that other sites were linking to.
avoid_when: The removed content is not genuinely similar or superior to your replacement content, or when the linked-to site is still active and has simply moved the content.
source_title: What Are Backlinks in SEO Why You Need Them Best Practices Part 2

name: Broken Link Building on Resource Pages
summary: Find niche resource pages with broken links using search strings and a Chrome extension, then contact site owners to report broken links and suggest your site as a replacement.
use_when: You have high-quality content relevant to a specific niche and want to earn backlinks by providing genuine value to site owners who have broken links on their resource pages.
avoid_when: Your content does not genuinely match the topic of the broken link, or when you intend to request a link without first providing value by reporting the broken link.
source_title: What Are Backlinks in SEO Why You Need Them Best Practices Part 2

name: Blogger Outreach for Niche Backlink Acquisition
summary: Identify relevant niche bloggers, filter out non-blog content, and reach out with a free product offer (without requesting links) to earn editorial backlinks.
use_when: You have a product or service and want to build backlinks by partnering with niche bloggers who write about topics relevant to your offering.
avoid_when: Your outreach message explicitly requests a link in exchange for a product or review, which violates Google's Webmaster Guidelines and risks a manual penalty.
source_title: What Are Backlinks in SEO Why You Need Them Best Practices Part 4

name: Link Reclamation from Unlinked Brand Mentions
summary: Use brand monitoring tools to find existing online mentions of your brand that do not include a backlink, then contact the authors to request a link addition.
use_when: Your brand is already being mentioned online by third-party publishers or bloggers, but those mentions lack a hyperlink back to your website.
avoid_when: The brand mention is on a low-quality, spammy, or irrelevant site where a backlink would provide no SEO value or could be harmful.
source_title: What Are Backlinks in SEO Why You Need Them Best Practices Part 4

name: Journalist Outreach for Backlink Acquisition
summary: Register as an expert source on journalist query platforms, respond to relevant queries with credentials and insights, and earn backlinks through media coverage.
use_when: When building domain authority through earned media and you have subject matter expertise to offer journalists seeking expert sources.
avoid_when: When you lack genuine expertise in your niche or cannot commit to monitoring and responding to journalist queries regularly.
source_title: What Are Backlinks in SEO Why You Need Them Best Practices Part 5

name: Competitive Backlink Analysis and Outreach Targeting
summary: Analyze competitor backlink profiles to discover niche-specific link opportunities such as podcast appearances, then systematically pursue those same sources.
use_when: When you need to identify proven, niche-relevant link sources and want to replicate competitor link-building success rather than starting from scratch.
avoid_when: When competitors are in a different niche or their backlink profile is dominated by paid or low-quality links that should not be replicated.
source_title: What Are Backlinks in SEO Why You Need Them Best Practices Part 5

name: Quality Backlink Acquisition Strategy
summary: A prioritized approach to building a high-value backlink profile by focusing on authoritative, topically relevant, dofollow links from diverse domains rather than accumulating volume from low-quality sources.
use_when: When building or auditing a backlink strategy to improve SERP rankings, especially when deciding which link opportunities to pursue or reject.
avoid_when: When operating in a niche where topical relevance is extremely narrow and diverse domain acquisition is structurally limited, or when evaluating nofollow-only link opportunities that may still drive referral traffic.
source_title: What Are Backlinks in SEO Why You Need Them What Types of Backlinks are Valuable

name: Social Capital ROI Benchmarking Across Social Platforms
summary: A procedure for measuring and comparing how quickly content generates social capital (likes, comments, reactions, followers) across different social media platforms using test accounts and standardized content.
use_when: When you are an investor, employee, or strategist evaluating the relative effectiveness and demographic reach of different social media platforms, or when deciding where to allocate content distribution effort.
avoid_when: When you have only one platform to evaluate, when your content is highly platform-specific and cannot be standardized across networks, or when you lack the ability to maintain multiple test accounts.
source_title: Status as a Service StaaS Social Capital ROI Part 1

name: Link Bait Content Creation Playbook
summary: A structured process for creating content specifically designed to attract organic backlinks by leveraging data, timeliness, and influencer ego bait strategies.
use_when: When building a link acquisition strategy that relies on earning organic backlinks through content, rather than manual outreach or link exchanges. Best applied when you have the capacity to conduct original research or surveys and can publish content quickly around trending topics.
avoid_when: When you need immediate link results (organic link bait takes time to gain traction), or when operating in a niche with very few active content creators or influencers to share and cite your work.
source_title: What Is Link Bait Here s Everything You Need To Know Best Practices Part 2

name: Link Bait Content Creation Playbook
summary: A structured process for creating content specifically designed to attract backlinks by being highly shareable, visually impressive, and deeply valuable to a target audience.
use_when: When the primary goal is to earn backlinks at scale through content, particularly when building domain authority or launching a link-building campaign without relying on outreach-heavy tactics.
avoid_when: When the content goal is purely conversion-focused or time-sensitive (e.g., product launches, news articles) where link magnetism is secondary to direct audience action.
source_title: What Is Link Bait Here s Everything You Need To Know Best Practices Part 1

name: Link Bait Content Promotion Playbook
summary: A multi-channel promotional strategy for distributing link bait content to maximize visibility and earn legitimate backlinks through social sharing, targeted outreach, and blogger engagement.
use_when: After publishing link bait content (e.g., original research, data studies, viral resources) and needing to amplify reach, especially when the publisher has a small audience or limited social media following.
avoid_when: When the content is not genuinely valuable or link-worthy, or when using press releases for non-newsworthy content such as routine blog posts or product announcements.
source_title: What Is Link Bait Here s Everything You Need To Know Best Practices Part 4

name: Update to New Google Crawling Documentation References and User Agents
summary: Procedure for SEO practitioners and site owners to update their configurations and documentation references following Google's November 2025 crawling documentation migration and user agent changes.
use_when: When updating robots.txt files, crawl configurations, or internal SEO documentation after Google's November 2025 crawling documentation migration; when a site does not support old browser versions and needs the updated Google Read Aloud user agent.
avoid_when: When no crawling-related configurations or documentation references exist on the site; when changes to Google News crawling behavior via Googlebot-News directives are expected to affect the News tab (they do not).
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog November 2025

name: Control Google Special-Case Crawlers via robots.txt
summary: Configure robots.txt to properly manage Google's special-case crawlers (APIs-Google, AdsBot-Google-Mobile, AdsBot-Google, Mediapartners-Google) using their specific user agent tokens instead of relying on global wildcard rules.
use_when: When a site owner needs to allow or restrict access for Google's special-case crawlers, particularly when managing Google Ads quality checks, AdSense ad relevance, or Google APIs push notification delivery.
avoid_when: When attempting to control the Google-Safety crawler, as it completely ignores robots.txt rules regardless of configuration.
source_title: Google Special Case Crawlers

name: Verify Google User-Triggered Fetcher Identity
summary: Identify and verify whether an incoming fetch request originates from a legitimate Google user-triggered fetcher by checking IP ranges and reverse DNS patterns.
use_when: When a fetch request appears in server logs from an unknown or suspicious agent that may be a Google user-triggered fetcher (e.g., Google Site Verifier, Google Cloud RSS fetchers) and you need to confirm its legitimacy.
avoid_when: When verifying standard Googlebot crawlers, which use different IP ranges and DNS masks. Also avoid relying on this process as exhaustive, since the published list does not cover all user-triggered fetchers.
source_title: Google User Triggered Fetchers

name: Detecting Social Capital to Financial Capital Conversion
summary: A method for identifying and assigning tangible value to social capital by observing where it gets exchanged for financial capital on mature social networks and digital platforms.
use_when: When analyzing social network monetization models, influencer economics, or attempting to assign measurable value to social capital assets in digital ecosystems.
avoid_when: When dealing with early-stage or immature networks where exchange infrastructure has not yet developed, or when seeking precise quantitative measurement frameworks (social capital lacks agreed-upon measurement definitions).
source_title: Status as a Service StaaS The Social Capital   Financial Capital Exchange Part 1

name: Early Platform Social Capital Accumulation Playbook
summary: A procedure for maximizing social capital on social networks by joining early, mastering platform-specific proof of work requirements, and leveraging existing status from other platforms.
use_when: Launching a brand or personal presence on a new or emerging social network where competition for followers and attention is still relatively low, or when entering an established platform with transferable external status (e.g., celebrity, cross-platform fame).
avoid_when: Joining a mature, saturated social platform without significant exogenous social capital to import, as competition for attention will be significantly more intense and follower compounding effects will be harder to trigger.
source_title: Status as a Service StaaS Social Networks as ICO s Part 2

name: robots.txt Placement and Scope Configuration
summary: Correctly place robots.txt files to control crawler access across domains, subdomains, protocols, and ports according to Google's specification.
use_when: Setting up or auditing robots.txt files for a website, especially when the site uses multiple subdomains, non-standard ports, or multiple protocols (HTTP, HTTPS, FTP).
avoid_when: When trying to block crawlers at the subdirectory level — robots.txt cannot be placed in subdirectories and crawlers will not check for it there.
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Examples of valid robots txt URLs

name: Google robots.txt User-Agent Group Resolution
summary: Procedure for determining which robots.txt group applies to a specific Google crawler by finding the most specific user-agent match and merging applicable rules.
use_when: When configuring or debugging robots.txt files that contain multiple user-agent groups, especially when targeting specific Google crawlers (e.g., Googlebot, Googlebot-Image) alongside a global wildcard group.
avoid_when: When robots.txt contains only a single user-agent group or only a wildcard (*) group with no crawler-specific groups — no resolution logic is needed in those cases.
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Order of precedence for user agents

name: Buffer Paid Plan Subscription Setup
summary: Step-by-step process to upgrade from Buffer's free trial or free plan to a paid subscription, including billing configuration and VAT handling.
use_when: When a user's 14-day trial is ending, when they need more than 3 channels or 10 scheduled posts per channel, or when upgrading from the Free plan to a paid Buffer plan.
avoid_when: When the user intends to stay on the Free plan or when they only need 3 or fewer channels with 10 or fewer scheduled posts per channel.
source_title: Pricing What features are supported for each specific channel

name: Implement a Valid robots.txt File for Google Crawling
summary: A structured procedure for creating or correcting a robots.txt file to meet Google's technical format requirements, ensuring crawling directives are properly interpreted and site indexing is not negatively impacted.
use_when: When creating a new robots.txt file, auditing an existing one for compliance issues, or diagnosing crawling/indexing problems suspected to be caused by malformed robots.txt directives.
avoid_when: When the robots.txt file has already been validated against Google's specifications and no crawling anomalies are present.
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification File format

name: Abandoned Cart Flow Audit
summary: A systematic checklist-based audit process to diagnose and optimize abandoned cart email flows using RPR and conversion rate metrics, covering triggers, exclusions, content, and channel strategy.
use_when: An abandoned cart flow exists in Klaviyo or similar platform and shows low/declining RPR, inconsistent conversion rates, or has not been reviewed since initial setup.
avoid_when: No abandoned cart flow is live yet (build the flow first), or the store lacks an ecommerce platform integration (e.g., Shopify) that provides metric-based triggers.
source_title: The Complete Checklist to Audit Your Email Marketing Automation Flows   Klaviyo Example audit abando

name: Identify and Handle Google User-Triggered Fetchers
summary: Recognize and correctly handle all Google user-triggered fetcher User-Agent strings to ensure proper serving of content for Chrome Web Store, FeedFetcher, Google-Agent, Google Messages, NotebookLM, Pinpoint, GoogleProducer, Read-Aloud, and Site Verifier.
use_when: When configuring a web server, CDN, or robots.txt access rules to correctly identify, allow, or differentiate between Google's user-triggered fetchers versus automated crawlers — especially when serving feeds, previews, verification tokens, or extension metadata.
avoid_when: When dealing with Googlebot or other standard search crawlers that are not user-triggered; this playbook does not apply to core indexing crawlers.
source_title: Google User Triggered Fetchers Chrome Web Store

name: Competitor A/B Test and Ad Strategy Reverse Engineering
summary: A structured process to reverse engineer competitors' landing pages, A/B tests, paid keywords, and retargeting ads to identify validated marketing tactics worth testing in your own growth efforts.
use_when: When entering a new market or channel and needing validated hypotheses to test, when growth efforts are stalling and competitive benchmarking is needed, or when planning a paid search or social ad strategy from scratch.
avoid_when: When competitors are in a declining growth phase (indicated by steadily declining branded search volume), as their tactics may reflect failing strategies rather than validated wins.
source_title: Competitive Analysis 2

name: Buffer Free-to-Paid Plan Upgrade Procedure
summary: Step-by-step process for transitioning from Buffer's Free plan or 14-day trial to a paid subscription, including channel setup and billing configuration.
use_when: A user wants to upgrade from the Buffer Free plan or activate a paid subscription before the 14-day trial expires to avoid account downgrade and channel locking.
avoid_when: User intends to stay on the Free plan permanently with 3 or fewer channels and no more than 10 scheduled posts per channel.
source_title: Pricing What features are supported for each specific channel 2

name: B2B Demand Generation System Setup
summary: A structured process for building a demand generation system that converts high-intent buyers into qualified pipeline, rather than chasing volume metrics through paid channels alone.
use_when: Brand foundation and landing pages are already in place, sales and marketing alignment is needed, and the team is ready to move from lead volume optimization to pipeline quality optimization.
avoid_when: Brand has not yet been established, landing pages do not convert, or the organization is still in early awareness-building stages where demand infrastructure prerequisites are not met.
source_title: Demand Where Most B2B Marketing Stalls What demand is really responsible for

name: Implement a Valid robots.txt File for Google Crawlers
summary: Structure and write a robots.txt file that correctly controls Google crawler access using supported fields and syntax rules.
use_when: When setting up or auditing a robots.txt file to control Googlebot and other Google crawlers across a website.
avoid_when: When attempting to use crawl-delay directives or when trying to prevent Google from indexing a URL entirely (disallow only blocks crawling, not URL indexation).
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Syntax

name: Ecommerce Diagnostic Toolkit Content Review Procedure
summary: A sequential procedure for team members to consume and apply the latest education content from Common Thread Collective's rolling feed in order to improve the Ecommerce Diagnostic Toolkit.
use_when: When contributing to or iterating on the Ecommerce Diagnostic Toolkit and needing to stay current with the latest ecommerce insights, AI creative trends, and performance tracking developments released in the past ten days.
avoid_when: When the feed has not been updated recently or when the goal is historical research rather than current product improvement — the feed only surfaces content from the past ten days and is not a comprehensive archive.
source_title: The Latest Ecommerce Updates from Common Thread Collective

name: Marketing Channel Diversification and Decay Management
summary: A proactive procedure for managing the inevitable performance decline of marketing channels by rotating creative content, monitoring decay signals, and systematically diversifying into newer channels before saturation hits.
use_when: When a marketing channel's clickthrough rate or engagement metrics show consistent decline over multiple periods, or when planning a long-term marketing strategy that must remain resilient to channel saturation.
avoid_when: When a channel has just launched or is in early adoption phase where optimization and scaling efforts should take priority over diversification; avoid applying this when decay signals have not yet appeared.
source_title: The Law of Shitty Clickthroughs at andrewchen

name: Google robots.txt URL Path Matching Implementation
summary: Procedure for correctly implementing and interpreting robots.txt allow/disallow rules using path matching, wildcards, and conflict resolution as per Google's specification.
use_when: When configuring or auditing robots.txt rules that use path values, wildcards, or when multiple rules may apply to the same URL and conflict resolution is needed.
avoid_when: When dealing with robots.txt directives unrelated to path matching (e.g., crawl-delay, sitemap directives) or when targeting non-Googlebot crawlers that may not support wildcard syntax.
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification URL matching based on path values

name: Crawl Budget Management: Avoiding Common Mistakes
summary: A set of corrective actions for site owners and developers to properly manage Google crawl budget by eliminating misconceptions and applying accurate crawl controls.
use_when: When a site has crawl budget concerns, high server error rates, or when developers are configuring robots.txt and crawl controls for Googlebot.
avoid_when: When the site is small and newly launched with no crawl budget constraints, or when the primary concern is ranking signals unrelated to crawling.
source_title: Myths and facts about crawling 2

name: Configure robots.txt for Correct Google Crawl Behavior
summary: Ensure your robots.txt file is served with the correct HTTP status codes and without logical redirects so Google's crawlers process it as intended rather than defaulting to unrestricted or fully blocked crawling.
use_when: When setting up or auditing a robots.txt file to control Google's crawl access, especially when diagnosing unexpected crawl behavior or cache-related issues.
avoid_when: When the site has no robots.txt file by design and unrestricted crawling is acceptable, or when using a platform where HTTP headers and redirect behavior cannot be controlled.
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Handling of errors and HTTP status codes

name: Implement a robots.txt File for Google Crawlers
summary: Create and configure a robots.txt file to control which parts of your site Google's automated crawlers can access, following the Robots Exclusion Protocol (REP) specification.
use_when: When you need to restrict or manage Google crawler access to specific sections of your website, or when setting up a new site that requires crawl directives.
avoid_when: When trying to block user-controlled Google crawlers or safety crawlers (e.g., malware analysis tools), as REP does not apply to those crawler types.
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification What is a robots txt file

name: Engineering-for-Marketing Free Tool Customer Acquisition Playbook
summary: Build and deploy a free tool that delivers immediate value to prospects, establishing domain expertise and trust before any sales engagement — modeled on HubSpot's Website Grader approach.
use_when: You have engineering resources available, serve a B2B or SMB audience, and want to build a scalable top-of-funnel acquisition channel that generates qualified leads organically through genuine value delivery.
avoid_when: You lack engineering capacity to build a genuinely useful tool, your product domain does not lend itself to a demonstrable diagnostic or scoring mechanism, or your team intends to gate the tool's output behind email capture before delivering value.
source_title: Customer Acquisition Maximizing your Funnel   For Entrepreneurs Using Engineering for Marketing

name: Optimize Crawl Efficiency Without Wasting Crawl Budget
summary: A set of practices to improve how efficiently Google crawls your site by focusing on page speed and meaningful content changes, while avoiding common tactics that waste crawl resources.
use_when: When a site owner or SEO practitioner wants to improve Google's crawl efficiency, especially for large sites with frequently changing content or sites experiencing crawl budget concerns.
avoid_when: When the site has very few pages or is already being fully crawled without issues — optimization effort may not be warranted.
source_title: Myths and facts about crawling

name: Reduce Google Crawl Rate
summary: Step-by-step procedure to reduce Google's crawl rate when it is causing server overload, unexpected infrastructure costs, or outages.
use_when: Google's crawler is causing critical server load, unexpected hosting costs, or site instability due to unusually high crawl frequency.
avoid_when: You want to increase crawl rate or when crawl rate is normal and the issue is a different type of traffic (e.g., bots, DDoS). Also avoid when the root cause is unrelated to crawling (e.g., misconfigured caching).
source_title: Reduce Google Crawl Rate

name: External Link Qualification and Attribution Playbook
summary: A procedure for correctly applying link attributes (sponsored, nofollow, ugc) to external links based on link type, ensuring compliance with Google's external linking best practices.
use_when: When adding external links to a website, including paid/sponsored links, user-generated content areas (forums, Q&A), or standard editorial outbound links.
avoid_when: When dealing with internal links between pages on the same domain — this playbook applies only to external outbound links.
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google External links link to other sites

name: SEO Link Anchor Text Implementation
summary: Implement crawlable, descriptive anchor text for both text and image links so Google can understand and index linked pages effectively.
use_when: Adding or auditing internal/external links on a webpage where anchor text must be crawlable and descriptive for Google Search.
avoid_when: Links are purely decorative with no SEO intent, or when the page is intentionally blocked from Google crawling via robots.txt or noindex.
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google Anchor text placement

name: B2B Brand Motion Implementation Playbook
summary: A repeatable process for building a sustainable brand motion in B2B SaaS that lowers CAC, accelerates sales cycles, and increases win rates by establishing buyer belief before sales contact.
use_when: When a B2B company has clear goals, a defined budget, a team to execute, and leadership buy-in to invest in brand as a long-term growth lever rather than a one-off campaign.
avoid_when: When there is no leadership alignment on brand as a long-term investment, no budget or dedicated team, or when the company is looking for short-term campaign results rather than foundational brand building.
source_title: Brand The Most Misunderstood Motion in B2B Marketing

name: B2B Story Archetype Selection and Commitment Playbook
summary: A process for B2B brands to select a single story archetype and commit to it consistently across all messaging, creative, and buyer experience to drive recognition and demand.
use_when: A B2B brand is defining or refining its market positioning, launching a new messaging strategy, or struggling with inconsistent brand recognition across campaigns and channels.
avoid_when: The brand is in a short-term campaign mode seeking quick tone shifts, or when leadership wants to experiment with multiple brand personalities simultaneously across different campaigns.
source_title: Story Archetypes That Drive Demand 2

name: Ecommerce Core Metrics Tracking Setup
summary: A required monitoring procedure for ecommerce businesses to track CLV, ROAS, and AOV in order to sustain growth, optimize ad spend, and maintain competitiveness.
use_when: When operating or auditing an ecommerce business that runs paid advertising campaigns and wants to make informed decisions about customer acquisition, retention, and marketing effectiveness.
avoid_when: When the business has no paid advertising spend and no repeat-purchase customer base, making ROAS and CLV largely irrelevant to operations.
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 2

name: Make Links Crawlable by Google
summary: Implement HTML links in a format that Google's crawlers can reliably parse, extract, and follow to discover new pages and understand anchor text.
use_when: When building or auditing a website where internal or external links must be discoverable by Google for crawling and indexing purposes.
avoid_when: When links are intentionally meant to be excluded from Google's crawl (e.g., nofollow, login-gated content, or JavaScript-only SPAs where crawlability is not a concern).
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google Make your links crawlable

name: Configure Google Crawl Controls and Monitor Site Health
summary: A step-by-step process for site owners to manage how Google crawls and uses their content, including controlling AI training data usage and diagnosing crawl issues via Search Console.
use_when: When a site owner wants to control whether their content is used to train Google AI models, monitor crawling activity, or diagnose server/speed issues related to Googlebot.
avoid_when: When the site has no robots.txt access or the owner lacks the ability to modify server-level configuration files; also avoid if the goal is to affect Search rankings, as Google-Extended is not a ranking signal.
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling Our standard crawlers always respect websites choices abo

name: Google Crawl Access and Control Implementation
summary: Configure how Google's crawlers interact with your site, including managing crawl frequency, blocking unwanted content, submitting new content, and handling subscription/paywalled pages properly.
use_when: When setting up a new site for crawling, managing crawl budget on large sites, implementing subscription/paywall content, or needing granular control over what Google indexes.
avoid_when: When your site has no crawl issues and default crawl behavior is acceptable, or when you do not have permission to access subscription/paywall pages on behalf of crawlers.
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the 2

name: Submit Sitemap to Influence Google Crawl Frequency
summary: Use sitemap files to signal new and updated pages to Googlebot, helping Google discover content faster and recrawl more frequently.
use_when: When a site has new or frequently updated pages that need to be discovered and indexed quickly by Google, or when crawl frequency is insufficient for freshness needs.
avoid_when: When a site has not changed in a long time and does not produce new content — sitemaps will have limited impact on crawl frequency in that scenario.
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the

name: Build a Social Media Strategy from Scratch
summary: A systematic procedure for establishing and executing an effective social media marketing strategy across platforms, from profile setup to analytics-driven optimization.
use_when: A business or creator is starting a new social media presence or restructuring an existing one to drive authentic engagement and measurable growth.
avoid_when: Looking for quick-growth shortcuts, bot-driven follower acquisition, or a one-size-fits-all approach applied identically across all platforms.
source_title: Rochi Zalani   Buffer Blog Posts

name: SEO Anchor Text Optimization Playbook
summary: A step-by-step process for writing effective, Google-compliant anchor text that helps users navigate and helps Google understand linked pages.
use_when: When creating internal or external links on any page you want Google to crawl and index, especially when writing new content or auditing existing links for SEO quality.
avoid_when: When links are purely navigational UI elements (e.g., breadcrumbs, pagination) where descriptive anchor text is not contextually appropriate, or when anchor text is auto-generated by a CMS.
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google Write good anchor text

name: Verify Google Crawler/Fetcher Requests via DNS Lookup
summary: A two-stage DNS verification process (reverse then forward lookup) to confirm that an HTTP request claiming to be from Google actually originates from Google infrastructure.
use_when: You suspect spammers or malicious actors are accessing your server while spoofing Google crawler identity, or when you need to programmatically confirm a request is genuinely from Googlebot or another Google fetcher.
avoid_when: When performing large-scale or high-frequency IP verification — in that case, use an automated solution matching against Google's published CIDR IP address list instead of per-request DNS lookups.
source_title: Verify Requests from Google Crawlers and Fetchers

name: LinkedIn Organic Reach Strategy: Employee Advocacy as Distribution System
summary: Restructure LinkedIn B2B demand generation strategy to prioritize employee personal profiles as the primary organic distribution engine, while repositioning company pages to supporting roles.
use_when: A B2B company is over-relying on its LinkedIn company page for organic reach and demand generation, and is seeing weak engagement or distribution despite follower growth
avoid_when: The primary LinkedIn objective is paid amplification or brand credibility anchoring, where company pages are the appropriate primary vehicle
source_title: Employee Profiles Outperform Brand Pages on LinkedIn Why we ran this analysis again and what changed

name: Calculate and Evaluate Returning Customer Rate for Ecommerce
summary: A step-by-step procedure for calculating the returning customer rate and interpreting results to improve customer retention strategy.
use_when: When a D2C ecommerce store owner wants to assess customer loyalty, diagnose retention problems, or evaluate the effectiveness of retention initiatives.
avoid_when: When the store is too new to have meaningful repeat purchase data or when the customer base is too small to produce statistically reliable results.
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 6

name: Ecommerce Marketing Efficiency Metrics Calculation Playbook
summary: A step-by-step process for calculating and tracking core ecommerce marketing efficiency metrics (MER, BEROAS, revenue per visitor, and sales by source) to evaluate channel performance and campaign effectiveness.
use_when: When a D2C ecommerce business needs to evaluate overall marketing spend efficiency, understand channel-level performance, or diagnose whether ad campaigns are profitable or breaking even.
avoid_when: When the business lacks reliable revenue and spend data across channels, or when tracking infrastructure is not yet in place to attribute sales by source accurately.
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 5

name: Ecommerce Ad Spend Metrics Calculation and Optimization Playbook
summary: Calculate CPC, CPM, and CPA for ad campaigns, interpret results in context, and adjust spend and targeting to improve marketing ROI.
use_when: When evaluating the cost-effectiveness of advertising campaigns, diagnosing underperforming ad spend, or optimizing budget allocation across platforms and audiences in ecommerce.
avoid_when: When campaign data is too early-stage or sample sizes are too small to produce statistically meaningful click, impression, or acquisition counts.
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 4

name: Buffer Social Media Management Platform Onboarding and Content Workflow
summary: Onboard to Buffer and execute a full social media management workflow including scheduling, engagement, analytics, and team approval across 11 platforms.
use_when: When a business or individual needs to set up a centralized social media management workflow across multiple platforms including Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, Threads, Bluesky, YouTube Shorts, Pinterest, Google Business, Mastodon, and X.
avoid_when: When managing fewer than 2 social platforms or when the user does not consent to receiving emails from Buffer, as email agreement is a hard requirement for account creation.
source_title: Buffer Social media management for everyone 3

summary: A step-by-step process to calculate and track core ecommerce marketing efficiency metrics including MER, breakeven ROAS, and revenue per visitor to evaluate channel and campaign performance.

name: Implement robots.txt Rules for Crawler Access Control
summary: Configure a robots.txt file to control how search engine crawlers access specific parts of a website, including blocking directories, allowing specific crawlers, and restricting file types.
use_when: When a site owner needs to restrict or grant crawler access to specific pages, directories, file types, or limit access to only certain crawlers (e.g., blocking staging directories, protecting private sections, or allowing only Googlebot-News).
avoid_when: When the goal is to prevent URLs from being indexed entirely — robots.txt disallows crawling but does not guarantee pages won't be indexed. Use noindex meta tags or HTTP headers for true de-indexing.

name: B2B Expand Motion Implementation

summary: A sequential process for setting up and monitoring key ecommerce performance metrics to optimize campaigns, control ad spend, and drive informed growth decisions.

summary: A structured process for calculating and interpreting key ecommerce advertising metrics (CPM, CPA, MER) and using traffic data to optimize marketing campaigns across channels.
use_when: When evaluating the effectiveness of paid advertising campaigns, diagnosing underperforming marketing channels, or making budget allocation decisions across ecommerce channels.

name: Ecommerce KPI Selection and Implementation Framework
summary: A structured process for selecting, creating, and monitoring the right ecommerce KPIs aligned to company growth stage and strategic objectives, avoiding metric overload.
use_when: When an ecommerce business needs to establish or reset its KPI framework, especially when feeling overwhelmed by too many metrics or when metrics no longer reflect business reality.

name: Ecommerce Metrics Tracking Setup and Review Cadence
summary: A structured procedure for selecting, segmenting, and periodically reviewing the ecommerce metrics that most directly impact profitability, avoiding vanity metrics and impulsive reactions to short-term data.
use_when: When launching a new D2C ecommerce brand, restructuring an existing metrics dashboard, or when current tracking feels unfocused or disconnected from profitability outcomes.
avoid_when: When making rapid tactical decisions based on daily fluctuations, or when the business lacks sufficient transaction volume to make conversion rate and CLV data statistically meaningful.

summary: A structured approach to tracking ecommerce performance metrics at appropriate frequencies, covering traffic, conversions, and retention to maintain business health and detect issues in real time.
